channel.title: The Truth about Jesus 關於耶穌的真相
channel.description: ...things into which angels long to look. (1 Peter 1:12 ESV) -- in traditional Chinese and English. 天使也渴想能知道一點。 (1 Peter 1:12 Chinese NET (T)) -- 用繁體中文和英語。

item.title: 41 - Reflection 思慮
item.description: Peter thinks about what happened 彼得思考發生了什麼
item.content:encoded:
Some time after the young men had carried out Sapphira's lifeless body,

在年輕人帶走薩菲拉毫無生氣的身體一段時間後，


Peter contemplated the day's shocking events.

彼得思考了當天令人震驚的事件。


Yes, it had been

是的，確實如此


a serious matter

一件嚴肅的事情


that the two had conspired to lie

兩人密謀撒謊


to the Holy Spirit! --

給聖靈！--


but

但是


death?!

處決？！


Everyone present

每個人都在場


and all who'd heard

以及所有聽過的人


had been terrified.

被嚇壞了。


💠



Peter had himself been taken by surprise

彼得自己也大吃一驚


when Ananias had collapsed and died.

當阿納尼亞斯倒下並去世時。


Of course, he had then expected Sapphira to suffer a similar fate,

當然，他當時預計薩菲拉會遭受類似的命運，


once it was established

一旦它建立起來


the two were equally in agreement

兩人同樣一致


to deceive.

欺騙。


💠



After a while, he remembered what Jesus had said

過了一會兒，他想起了耶穌說過的話


when the Pharisees had been scoffing at Him and loudly attributing

當法利賽人嘲笑他並大聲歸因於


to demonic activity! --

惡魔活動！--


the wonderful healing miracles given by God:

上帝賜予的奇妙的治癒奇蹟：


"He that is not with Me

「誰不與我相合的人


is against Me;

反對我；


and he that does not gather with Me

以及不與我收集的人


actively scatters.

積極撒。


Whoever speaks a word against the Son of Man

誰說一句話反對人子


will be forgiven:

將被原諒：


but whoever speaks against the Holy Spirit

但誰說出反對聖靈


will not be forgiven,

不會被原諒，


neither in this world,

在這個世界上也沒有，


nor in the world to come."

也不是在來世。」


💠



Based on Acts 5:1-11; Matthew 12:22-32.

基於使徒行傳5:1-11；馬太福音12:22-32。


💠

item.title: 40 - Sapphira 薩菲拉
item.description: Ananias' wife meets with Peter 亞拿尼亞的妻子與彼得會面
item.content:encoded:
Three hours later, after the young men had removed her husband's body,

三小時後，在那些年輕男人取出她丈夫的屍體後，


Sapphira arrived,

薩菲拉到了，


unaware

不知道的


of her newfound

她剛剛的


widowhood.

喪偶。


"Tell me, Sapphira," said the Holy Spirit through Peter gently,

「告訴我，薩菲拉，」聖靈透過彼得溫柔地說，


"this money which your husband brought us this morning --

「你丈夫今天早上給我們帶來的這筆錢——


was it the full amount you received from the sale of your field?"

這是你從出售你的田地中獲得的全部金額嗎？」


"Yes, of course it was," she replied quickly, with a big smile, wondering why the shekels were still lying there.

「是的，當然是這樣，」她快速地回答，帶著燦爛的笑容，想知道為什麼錢仍然躺在那裡。


Surely they were grateful to have received such a generous gift!

當然，他們很感激收到如此慷慨的禮物！


"The full amount?" Peter pressed.

「全額？」 彼得追問。


Why did he keep asking? And where was Ananias?

他為什麼一直問？ 阿納尼亞斯在哪裡？


"It was the total sum we received from the sale of our field," she insisted. "We sold it to our neighbour, Abel."

「這是我們從出售田地中獲得的總金額，」她堅持說。 「我們把它賣給了我們的鄰居亞伯。」


Foolish Abel, she smirked inwardly. I still can't believe he gave us such a ridiculous price!

愚蠢的亞伯，她內心咧嘴一笑。 我仍然不敢相信他給了我們如此荒謬的價格！


💠



"Since you and your husband have agreed together

「既然你和你丈夫已經同意了


as equals

作為平等夥伴


to test the Spirit of the Lord," replied the Holy Spirit

考驗主的靈，」聖靈回答道


sorrowfully through Peter,

透過彼得悲傷地，


"Look!"

「看！」


Sapphira looked up and saw some of the young men entering the room,

薩菲拉抬頭一看，看到一些年輕男人走進了房間，


their eyes widening

他們睜大了眼睛


as they saw her smiling at them.

當他們看到她對他們微笑時。


"The feet of those who buried your husband are at the door,

「埋葬你丈夫的那些人的腳在門口，


and they will carry you out."

他們會把你抬出去。」


She collapsed

她摔倒了


in a heap.

在地上。


💠



Based on Acts 5:1-11.

基於使徒行傳5:1-11。


💠

item.title: 39 - Ananias 阿納尼亞斯
item.description: Ananias brings money to the Apostles 阿納尼亞斯給使徒們帶來了錢
item.content:encoded:
At last, it was his turn.

終於輪到他了。


Ananias stepped forward and laid the shekels --

阿納尼亞斯走上前去，放下了錢——


half of what he had received from selling a field to his foolish neighbour! --

那隻是他把田地賣給愚蠢鄰居所得金額的一半！--


at the Apostle Peter's feet.

在使徒彼得的腳下。


"We sold a field, and offer the full proceeds to support the needy among the Messiah's followers," he said,

他說，「我們賣掉了一片田地，現在，我們將全部收益捐給您，用於幫助彌賽亞信徒中的貧困者。」


as Joe Barnabas had done.

正如喬·巴納巴斯所做的那樣。


That ought to buy him a little favour and influence with the leaders of the Messiah's movement!

這應該讓他在彌賽亞運動的主管人中獲得一點恩惠和影響力！


💠



The Holy Spirit bristled, filling Peter with honest indignation,

聖靈怒髮衝冠，讓彼得充滿了真誠的憤慨，


then spoke gently through him.

然後透過他悄然說話。


"Ananias, how has Satan so filled your heart

「阿納尼亞斯，撒旦是如何填滿你的心的


to lie to the Holy Spirit,

對聖靈撒謊，


and conceal a portion

並隱藏一部分


of the proceeds of the sale?"

銷售收益？」


The amount was not the question,

金額不是問題，


as Peter well knew

正如彼得所熟知的那樣


from his Master's teaching on giving.

源自於他師父關於給予錢的教導。


But the lie

但是謊言


was a problem.

是個問題。


💠



"Wasn't the field yours

「田地不是你的嗎


before it was sold?

在它被賣掉之前？


And once sold, the money received was still yours -- to do with as you pleased.

一旦售出，收到的錢仍然是你的——隨心所欲。


How could you think to do such a thing?

你怎麼會想到做這種事？


You haven't lied to men,

你沒有對男人撒謊，


but to God!"

但卻對上帝說謊！」


As soon as the words were out of Peter's mouth,

彼得一說出這些話，


Ananias

阿納尼亞斯


collapsed.

摔倒了。


💠



Based on Acts 4:32-37; Acts 5:1-11; Mark 12:41-44.

基於使徒行傳4:32-37；使徒行傳5:1-11；馬可福音12:41-44。


💠

item.title: 38 - The scheme 狡計
item.description: A married couple plan to deceive 一對已婚夫婦計劃欺騙
item.content:encoded:
"People are selling houses and fields,

「人們正在出售房屋和田地，


and bringing the whole proceeds

並帶來全部收入


to the Apostles," remarked Ananias to his wife,

給使徒們，」阿納尼亞斯對他的妻子說，


a troubled look on his face.

他臉上露出憂心忡忡的表情。


The couple were two of many in Jerusalem

這對夫婦是耶路撒冷眾多人中的兩個人


who had come to believe in Jesus,

開始信奉耶穌，


the resurrected Messiah.

復活的彌賽亞。


"Oh...!"

「哦......！」


Sapphira pondered, then said reluctantly:

薩菲拉沉思著，然後不情願地說：


"I suppose we ought to do the same..."

「或許我們也應該這樣做......」


💠



"Look how much I got for our field!"

「看看我為我們的田地得到了多少錢！」


Ananias came in,

阿納尼亞斯進來了，


his hands filled with shekels.

他手裡拿著滿滿的錢。


"Old Abel wanted to expand his farm,

「老亞伯想擴大他的農場，


and I pretended we were reluctant to sell...

我假裝我們不願意賣......


and look at the bargain I made!"

看看我做的討價還價！」


He dumped the shekels on the table,

他把錢倒在桌上，


then pulled even more out of his pockets,

然後從他的口袋裡掏出更多，


as Sapphira looked on, astonished.

薩菲拉驚訝地看著。


"You always were a hard bargainer," she laughed in amazement.

「你一直都很會討價還價，」她笑驚豔說。。


"Why, this is more than double what that field is worth!"

「嗬，這是那個領域價值的兩倍多！」


💠



Ananias savoured his wife's praise, then pondered a moment.

阿納尼亞斯品味著妻子的讚美，然後思考了一會兒。


"You know, I think it would be quite believable

「你知道，我認為這很可信


if we only gave half...

如果我們只給一半......


Just the other day I saw Joe Barnabas

就在前幾天，我看到了喬·巴納巴斯


bring in the proceeds of the sale of his field,

帶來出售他田地的收益，


and it was only..." he named a paltry sum.

這只是......」他說出了一個小的金額。


"That's all?!"

「僅此而已？！」


Sapphira shook her head in disbelief.

薩菲拉難以置信地搖了搖頭。


Joe Barnabas did not have one-tenth the business acumen of her husband!

喬·巴納巴斯的商業頭腦沒有她丈夫的十分之一！


💠



Based on Acts 4:32-37; Acts 5:1-2.

基於使徒行傳4:32-37；使徒行傳5:1-2。

item.title: 37 - Released 釋放
item.description: The Apostles head home 使徒們回家
item.content:encoded:
As Peter, John and the other Apostles headed back to their place in Jerusalem,

當彼得、約翰和其他使徒還在返回耶路撒冷住所的路上，


their backs bruised and egos crushed

他們的背部受傷，自尊心被擊碎


by the flogging they'd endured before the Ruling Council,

透過他們在統治理事會面前忍受的鞭打，


the Holy Spirit spoke the words of the Master

聖靈講了大師的話


gently yet clearly

溫柔而清晰


into their hearts.

進入他們的心。


💠



"If the world hates you,

「世界若恨你們，


keep in mind: it hated Me first.

你們該知道它先恨我。


If you belonged to the world,

你們若屬世界，


it would love you as its own.

世界會像愛自己的孩子一樣愛你。


But you do not belong to the world, for i've chosen you out of the world;

可是你們不屬世界，因為我從世界中揀選了你們出來；


this is why the world hates you.

這就是世界討厭你的原因。


Remember the word i spoke to you:

要記得我從前對你們所說的話：


'No servant is greater than his master.'

『奴僕不可能大過主人。』


If they persecuted Me,

如果他們迫害我，


they will persecute you also."

他們也會迫害你。」


💠



This is how they treated our Lord, realized John, his heart filling instantly with an unfathomable joy.

這就是他們對待我們的主的方式，約翰意識到，他的心立刻充滿了深不可測的喜悅。


As He is, so also are we

耶穌如何，我們也如何


in this world!

在這世上！


A wide grin began to spread across his face.

他臉上露出了燦爛的笑容。


💠



At that moment, Peter turned to him and the others,

那一刻，彼得轉向他和其他人，


and spoke the words

並說出了這些話


the Holy Spirit was bringing alive in his heart:

聖靈使這些話語在他心中活了過來：


"Blessed are you, when they criticize you harshly,

「你有福了，當他們嚴厲地批評你時，


persecute and slander you,

迫害你們、揑造各樣壞話毀謗你們，


because of Me.

因我。


Rejoice and be glad: you have a great reward in Heaven! --

應當歡喜快樂，因為你們在天上的賞賜是大的！——


for in the same way they persecuted

因為人也以同樣的方式迫害


the prophets who were before you!"

在你們以前的先知！」


💠



In spite of their tiredness, hunger, bruises and humiliation, the Apostles now sang --

儘管他們疲憊、飢餓、瘀傷和羞辱，使徒們現在唱著——


some even danced! --

有些人甚至跳舞了！——


all the way home.

一路走回家。


They had been counted worthy to suffer

他們被認為是值得受苦的


for the Name of their crucified Lord:

因為了他們被釘十字架的主的名：


Jesus.

耶穌。


💠



The very next day -- and every day after that --

第二天——以及之後的每一天——


they were back in the Temple --

他們回到了聖殿——


and in the house --

並且在房子裡——


teaching the hungry

教飢餓的人


about Jesus,

關於耶穌，


their risen Messiah.

他是他們復活的彌賽亞。


💠



Based on Acts 5:40-42; John 15:18-21; 1 John 4:17; Matthew 5:11-12.

基於使徒行傳5:40-42；約翰福音15:18-21；約翰一書4:17；馬太福音5:11-12。


💠

item.title: 36 - Decision 決定
item.description: The Chief Priest renders his decision 祭司長做出了決定
item.content:encoded:
Both Nicodemus and Joseph breathed grateful prayers of relief

尼哥底母和約瑟都鬆了一口氣，滿懷感恩地禱告


as Gamaliel's calming influence began to take effect across the Council Hall.

當迦瑪列的鎮靜影響開始在整個理事會大廳生效時。


Could he possibly be

他有可能是


a secret believer in their Lord?

主的秘密信徒嗎？


He had practically admitted

他幾乎已經承認了


Jesus might have been sent by God!

耶穌可能是上帝派來的！


Many in the Council Hall were murmuring and nodding in agreement --

理事會大廳裡許多人低聲交談，點頭表示贊同——


could they also

他們也可以


be on the verge of faith?

處於信仰的邊緣嗎？


💠



Although irreversibly affronted by the Apostles' bold words,

雖然他永遠無法原諒使徒們那些大膽的言論，


Caiaphas sensed the wind of opinion shifting

該亞法感覺到了輿論的轉變之風


toward Gamaliel's completely reasonable counsel --

朝著迦瑪列完全合理的建議——


and chose to go with the flow.

並選擇隨波逐流。


The Chief Priest cleared his throat.

祭司長清了清嗓子。


"In view of our esteemed brother Gamaliel's wise advice,

「鑑於我們尊敬的兄弟迦瑪列的明智建議，


I recommend we have these men flogged,

我建議我們鞭打這些人，


command them again not to teach in the name of the false Messiah, and --"

再次命令他們不要以假彌賽亞的名義教導，並且——」


he continued reluctantly,

他不情願地繼續說，


"release them."

「釋放他們。」


💠



Although for the moment it was expedient

雖然目前它是權宜之計


to go along with the Council's majority opinion,

同意理事會的多數意見，


the Chief Priest was not in the least convinced by Gamaliel's argument.

祭司長絲毫沒有被迦瑪列的論點說服。


This troublesome movement defied all norms! --

這個麻煩的運動違背了所有規範！--


and threatened to derail everything they had worked so hard to establish

並有可能毀掉他們辛苦建立的一切


under the Roman Empire.

在羅馬帝國統治下。


Caiaphas had no doubt

該亞法毫不懷疑


they would be seeing these men again.

他們會再次見到這些人。


And when they did,

當這種情況發生時，


he would do everything in his power

他會盡他所能


to ensure their swift demise

為了確保他們迅速消亡


and eradicate this troublesome movement.

並根除這場麻煩的運動。


💠



Based on John 12:42-43; Acts 5:33-40; John 15:20-21.

基於約翰福音12:42-43；使徒行傳5:33-40；約翰福音15:20-21。

item.title: 35 - Gamaliel 迦瑪列
item.description: Gamaliel speaks to the Council 迦瑪列向理事會講話
item.content:encoded:
Just as Nicodemus and Joseph began to despair for the lives of their Lord's Apostles...

就像尼哥底母和約瑟夫開始對他們主使徒的生命感到絕望一樣......


one of the Council,

理事會的一名成員，


a well-known and highly respected teacher of the Law

一位著名且備受尊敬的法律教師


named Gamaliel,

名為迦瑪列，


who had remained seated while the others were expressing their outrage,

當其他人表達他們的憤怒時，他仍然坐著，


rose calmly from his place.

平靜地從他的地方站起來。


A hush began to fall over the Council Hall,

理事會大廳開始陷入寂安靜，


as Gamaliel signalled the Temple guards to remove the Apostles from the room.

當迦瑪列向聖殿衛士發出訊號，他們把使徒們帶出房間。


Then,

然後，


when all was quiet,

當一切人都安靜的時候，


he spoke.

他說話了。


💠



"Men, Israelites, take a moment

「男人，以色列人，花點時間


and think carefully

並仔細思考


about what you ought to do with these men."

關於你應該對這些人做什麼。」


A few who were standing

有幾個站著的人


began to resume their seats.

決定坐下。


Gamaliel continued:

迦瑪列繼續說道：


"We've dealt with false messiahs before: remember Theudas?

「我們以前處理過虛假的彌賽亞：還記得特烏達斯嗎？


He made great claims for himself, and about four hundred followed him.

他對自己吹噓得非常厲害，進而大約有四百人跟隨他。


But when he was killed, all his followers were dispersed

但當他被殺時，他所有的追隨者都分散了


and the movement came to nothing."

他的運動最終化為烏有。」


More sat now, remembering.

更多人坐了下來，記得。


"And after him, Judas from Galilee rose to prominence, in the days of the head tax enrollment; he attracted many followers!"

「在他之後，來自加利利的猶大在頭稅登記的日子裡嶄露頭角；他吸引了許多追隨者！」


He looked around.

他環顧四周。


"But when he died, all of his followers were scattered, too."

「但當他去世時，他所有的追隨者也散了。」


Heads nodded in sage agreement now, as nearly all were back in their seats.

現在大多數人都明智地點頭表示贊同，在幾乎所有人都回到了座位上。


💠



"So now, I say to you:

「所以現在，我對你說：


Desist from these men, and leave them be.

遠離這些人，讓他們一個人待著。


For if this movement is from man,

因為如果這個運動來自人類，


it will come to an end and disappear."

它將結束並消失。」


Many of the Council felt calmer now -- even Caiaphas! --

許多理事會成員現在感覺更平靜了——即使是該亞法！——


at the thought of the false Messiah's movement

一想到假彌賽亞的運動


dissolving on its own.

自行溶解。


"But if it is from God, you will not be able to stop it.

「但如果它來自上帝，你將無法阻止它。


You will only find yourselves

你們只會發現自己


fighting

戰鬥


against

反抗


God."

上帝。」


💠



Based on Acts 5:33-39.

基於使徒行傳5:33-39。


💠

item.title: 34 - Fury 暴怒
item.description: The Holy Spirit speaks; the Council reacts 聖靈說話，理事會做出反應
item.content:encoded:
"We must obey God rather than men,"

「我們必須服從上帝，而不是人，」


the Holy Spirit explained patiently to the Council through Peter,

聖靈透過彼得耐心地向理事會解釋，


then gently, yet clearly, through Thomas:

然後溫和地，但清晰地，透過托馬斯：


"The God of our fathers has resurrected Jesus

「我們祖先的上帝復活了耶穌


whom you killed

你殺了他


when you crucified Him on the Cross."

當你把他釘在十字架上時。」


John was next:

約翰是下一個：


"God has appointed Him

「上帝已經任命了他


Prince and Saviour,

王子和救世主，


and exalted Him to His right hand,

將他高舉在自己的右邊，


to give repentance and forgiveness of sins to Israel."

讓以色列悔改和赦免罪。」


And finally, through James:

最後，透過詹姆斯：


"We are witnesses of these things,

「我們是這些事情的見證人，


as is the Holy Spirit Himself,

就像聖靈本身一樣，


whom God has given

上帝賜予的靈


to those who believe in Him."

給那些相信他的人。」


💠



The nuance did not escape the Chief Priest, who leapt to his feet in fury.

細微差別並沒有逃脫首席牧師，他憤怒地跳了起來。


"Are you insinuating that we do not believe?

「你是在暗示我們不相信嗎？


We,

我們，


the guardians of the Law of Moses,

摩西律法的守護者，


the Temple,

聖殿，


and all the traditions of the elders?!"

以及長輩們的所有傳統？！」


He spoke as fiercely as he could,

他儘可能地激烈地說話，


as many other Council members, enraged, jumped to their feet.

當許多其他理事會成員憤怒地站起來時。


💠



As calls for execution -- even crucifixion! -- echoed throughout the Council Hall,

隨著處決的呼聲——甚至是釘十字架！——在整個理事會廳迴盪，


Nicodemus exchanged a worried glance with Joseph of Arimathea.

尼哥底母與亞利馬太的約瑟夫交換了擔憂的目光。


It was happening

它正在發生


again.

再次。


💠



Based on Acts 5: 27-33.

基於使徒行傳5：27-33。


💠

item.title: 33 - Hearing resumed 聽證會恢復
item.description: The Apostles again stand before the Ruling Council 使徒們再次站在統治理事會面前
item.content:encoded:
"Lock the doors!"

「把門鎖上！」


Caiaphas tried to keep the sense of urgency out of his voice

該亞法試圖將緊迫感從他的聲音中排除在外


as the entire Ruling Council hustled safely back into the Council Hall

當整個統治理事會安全地趕回理事會大廳時


with their unusually compliant prisoners.

和他們異常順從的囚犯。


No sense in taking any chances.

冒險沒有任何意義。


💠



As the Council settled back into their seats and rearranged their robes,

當理事會回到他們的座位上並重新安排他們的長袍時，


the Apostles stood quietly in the midst.

使徒們靜靜地站在中間。


They had not intended to escape...

他們本不打算逃跑......


but what else could one do --

但人們還能做什麼——


faced with an angel sent by God?

面對上帝派來的天使？


Deep down inside they had known:

內心深處，他們知道：


the Ruling Council,

統治理事會，


like a dog with a bone,

像一條有骨頭的狗，


would not give up so easily.

不會那麼容易放棄。


💠



Once he had sufficiently recovered from their bold venture, the Chief Priest bristled with indignation.

一旦他從他們大膽的冒險中充分恢復過來，首席牧師就充滿了憤慨。


"Did we not strictly charge you," he began severely, "not to teach anyone in this name?"

「我們不是嚴格命令你嗎，」他嚴厲地開始說，「不要以這個名義教任何人？」


He hated to think -- let alone speak -- the name of the false Messiah.

他討厭思考——更不用說說話了——假彌賽亞的名字。


"And now, look! You've filled Jerusalem with your teaching;

「現在，看！ 你用你的教誨填滿了耶路撒冷；


and you want to blame us

你想責怪我們


for the blood of this man!"

為了這個人的血！」


💠



As the Apostles looked quietly back at their accusers,

當使徒們靜靜地回頭看他們的指控者時，


enjoying the flow of supernatural peace through their hearts and minds,

透過他們的心靈和思想享受超自然和平的流動，


certain ones sensed the Holy Spirit rising within,

某些人感覺到聖靈在內心升起，


giving them the words

給他們話語


with which to respond.

回應。


💠



Based on Acts 5:17-28.

基於使徒行傳5:17-28。


💠

item.title: 6 - Epilogue 尾聲
item.description: A story of Christmas 聖誕節的故事
item.content:encoded:
John 1:1-3,14 (ESV)

1 In the beginning was the Word,

and the Word was with God,

and the Word was God.

2 He was in the beginning with God.


3 All things were made through him,

and without him was not any thing made that was made.


14 And the Word became flesh and dwelt among us,

and we have seen his glory,

glory as of the only Son from the Father, full of grace and truth.


John 1:1-3,14 (Chinese NET (T))

1 太初有道，道與 神同在，道就是 神。

2 這道在太初就與 神同在。


3 萬物都是藉着他造的；

凡被造的，沒有一樣不是藉着他造的。


14 道成了肉身，住在我們中間，

充充滿滿的有恩典有真理。

我們見過他的榮光，正是父獨生子的榮光。


John 1:18 (NIV)

18 No one has ever seen God,

but the one and only Son,

who is himself God

and is in closest relationship with the Father,

has made him known.


John 1:18 (Chinese NET (T))

18 從來沒有人見過 神，

只有與父最親近的獨生子將 神表明出來。


💠

item.title: 32 - Invitation 邀請
item.description: The Ruling Council invites the Apostles to come back 統治理事會邀請使徒們回來
item.content:encoded:
John looked up, then nudged Peter.

約翰抬起頭來，然後推了推彼得。


They and the other Apostles were sitting in the Temple, as their Master had done,

他們和其他使徒坐在聖殿裡，就像他們的主人所做的那樣，


speaking His words of life

把生命的道講給


to the people sitting around them --

給坐在他們周圍的人——


just as the angel

就像那個天使一樣


who'd released them from prison

誰把他們從監獄裡釋放的


had commanded.

已經命令了。


Peter looked up.

彼得抬起頭。


A large group of well-dressed elders and rulers -- actually, the entire Ruling Council! -- led by the Chief Priest and accompanied by guards and attendants,

一大群衣著整禮的長老和統治者——實際上是整個統治理事會！——祭司長在前面，由警衛和隨從陪同，


was heading their way.

正朝著他們的方向前進。


💠



"Excuse me."

「打擾一下。」


It galled the Chief Priest to have to speak so politely

祭司長不得不如此禮貌地說話，這讓他感到很惱大


to illiterate commoners,

給文盲的平民，


but he didn't dare risk the anger of the huge crowd surrounding them --

但他不敢冒著他們周圍一大群人的憤怒的風險——


some of whom were beginning to stand up --

他們中的一些人開始站起來了——


rather threateningly, Caiaphas thought.

相當具有威脅性，該亞法想。


"We would like to invite you, the chosen followers of --" he coughed, "Jesus of Nazareth, to a hearing of the Ruling Council,

「我們想邀請你們，被選中的追隨者——」他咳嗽道，「拿撒勒的耶穌，參加執政委員會的聽證會，


so we may discuss together,

所以我們可以一起討論，


in a civil, collaborative manner,

以文明、協作的方式，


what you are doing."

你在做什麼。」


💠



As the Holy Spirit nudged his heart to accede to the request of the man

當聖靈推動他的心同意這個人的請求時


who had only recently imprisoned

那個不久前才把他們關進監獄的人


him and the others

他和其他人


for days,

幾天來，


the words of the Master echoed

大師的話迴盪著


quietly but persistently

安靜但堅持不懈地


through John's mind:

透過約翰的頭腦：


"Do not resist an evil person.

「不要反抗惡人。


If someone slaps you on the right cheek,

若有人打你的右臉，


turn to him the other also.

讓他們也打你的另一邊臉頰。


If someone forces you to go one mile with him,

若有人強逼你走一里路，


go two.

你就同他走二里。


Whoever demands of you,

如果有人向你索取東西，


give to him;

就給他；


whoever wants to borrow from you,

有向你借貸的，


do not refuse."

不推辭。」


💠



"Love your enemies!

「愛你們的仇敵！


Bless the one who curses you!

祝福詛咒你的人！


Do good to the one who hates you.

善待恨你們的人


Pray for those

為那些的禱告


who lead you in chains

為用鎖鏈捆綁你們的人，


and persecute you."

為那些迫害你們的人。」


💠



Peter, John and the rest of the Apostles

彼得、約翰和其他使徒


got up quietly,

安靜地起床了，


and, without protest,

並且，沒有抗議，


followed the Ruling Council

跟隨統治理事會


back into the Council Hall.

回到理事會大廳。


💠



Based on Acts 5:17-26; Matthew 5:38-45; Luke 6:27-30.

基於使徒行傳5:17-26；馬太福音5:38-45；路加福音6:27-30。


💠

item.title: 5 - Birth 出生
item.description: Jesus is born 耶穌誕生
item.content:encoded:
Gabriel cleared his throat and raised his baton.

加布裡埃爾清了清嗓子，舉起了他的指揮棒。


The angelic hosts of Heaven stopped chatting and rustling

天堂的天使們停止了聊天和沙沙聲


and stood invisibly to attention in the darkened sky,

它們筆直地矗立在昏暗的夜空中，隱形無蹤，


as unsuspecting shepherds in the fields below

而下面田野裡的一些毫無猜的牧羊人


sat quietly together around a small fire,

一起安靜地圍著一小塊火，


their sheep huddled close in the snowy fields.

他們的羊群蜷縮在雪地裡。


💠



In the even darker stable,

在更黑暗的馬廄裡，


Mary perspired and gasped and groaned,

馬利亞渾身是汗，氣喘吁籲，呻吟，


as her body laboured to expel

當她的身體努力排出時


the holy, precious parcel within.

裡面神聖而珍貴的包裹。


💠



If He could have, Jesus would have cried out --

如果他可以的話，耶穌會喊——


but He couldn't.

但他做不到。


After a long, relaxing stay

經過長時間的放鬆停留


in a dark, cosy, comforting environment --

在一個黑暗、溫馨、舒適的環境中——


during which He had learned to kick and move about in a limited way,

在此期間，他學會了以有限的方式踢和移動，


His every need supplied through a fleshy tube connected to His little belly --

他的每一個需求都透過連線到他的小肚子的肉管來供應——


everything was changing!

一切都在改變！


The walls of His safe, soft, friendly home were closing in on Him,

他安全、柔軟、友好的家的牆壁正在逼近他，


squeezing Him,

擠壓他，


as if He were suddenly --

彷彿他突然——


no longer welcome.

不再歡迎。


He was going somewhere --

他要去某個地方——


His little head being bent out of shape!

他的小頭現在正被掰彎變形呢！


It was all so unexpected and terrifyingly strange,

這一切都出人意料，而且非常奇怪，


He didn't have time to ask His Father

他沒時間問他父親


what was happening.

到底是怎麼回事。


💠



The moment Jesus' head popped out,

耶穌的頭出來的那一刻，


Gabriel pointed at the specially designed star angel,

加布裡埃爾指著特別設計的星星天使，


who now rose brilliantly --

現在已經崛起，光芒四射——


twinkling with every colour of the rainbow!

閃爍著彩虹的每一種顏色！


He gave the momentous, joyous,

他給了重要的，快樂的，


reassuring

安慰的


announcement,

公告，


as shepherds and sheep

作為牧羊人和羊群


fell over one another

互相摔倒


in startled amazement.

驚愕不已。


Now Gabriel lifted his baton,

現在加布裡埃爾舉起了他的指揮棒，


and the full angelic choir appeared, infusing the entire sky with a glorious light,

完整的天使合唱團出現了，為整個天空注入了燦爛的光芒，


singing beautifully,

優美地唱歌，


melodiously,

旋律美妙地，


with unquenchable joy.

帶著不竭的喜悅。


💠



Meanwhile, in the darkened stable below,

與此同時，在下面黑暗的馬廄裡，


as soon as His tiny lungs

當他小小的肺


filled

吸入


with their first gasp of air,

第一口空氣時，


Baby Jesus...

耶穌寶寶......


cried.

哭了。


💠



Based on John 1:1-2,14; Luke 2:1-14; Matthew 2:1-2,9-10.

基於約翰福音1:1-2,14；路加福音2:1-14；馬太福音2:1-2,9-10。


💠

item.title: 4 - Incarnation 化身
item.description: Jesus becomes human 耶穌變成了人類
item.content:encoded:
Jesus was falling toward the darkened Earth

耶穌正向黑暗的地球墜落


and shrinking

並且縮小


at the same time.

同時。


It was a bizarre sensation

那是一種奇怪的感覺


to become less

變得更少


than He really was,

比他真正的，


but by faith

但憑著信心


He chose

他選擇了


to relax

放鬆


and receive.

並受。


With His Father's help

在他父親的幫助下


He would begin --

他將開始——


and complete --

並完成——


the most unthinkable transformation

最不可思議的轉變


anyone anywhere

任何地方的任何人


had ever undertaken.

曾經承擔過。


💠



Father!

父親！


i can't see anything!

我什麼都看不見！


Jesus could no longer speak aloud --

耶穌再也無法大聲說話了——


having divested Himself of His Godlike qualities --

祂捨棄了自己神一般的能力——


but He could still sense His Father's presence,

但他仍然能感覺到他父親的存在，


even now.

即使是現在。


Where am i?!

我在哪裡？！


Relax, My Son, came the instant reassurance.

放鬆，我的兒子，瞬間就放心了。


You're in Your mother's womb.

你在你母親的子宮裡。


Ohh...

哦......


After a while,

過了一會兒，


Jesus realized:

耶穌意識到：


it was oddly comforting in here...!

這裡出奇地讓人感到舒適……！


💠



Based on John 1:1-3,14-15,18; Luke 1:26-35; Matthew 1:18-25.

基於約翰福音1:1-3,14-15,18；路加福音1:26-35；馬太福音1:18-25。


💠

item.title: 3 - Ready 準備好
item.description: All Heaven is ready for Jesus' birth 整個天堂都為耶穌的誕生做好了準備
item.content:encoded:
At last, it was Time.

終於到了時間。


There was much hustle and bustle in Heaven,

天堂裡有很多熙熙攘攘，


as the entire angelic host added the final flourishes

當全體天使加入最後的點睛之筆時


to the glorious, melodious song they were to sing

他們要唱的那首光榮、悠美的歌曲


for a few shepherds and their sheep

為了幾個牧羊人和他們的羊


in a field at night...!

晚上在田野裡......！


"Really?!"

「真的嗎？！」


Gabriel had asked incredulously.

加百列不置信地問道。


"All this for a few shepherds and some sheep, my Lord?!"

「這一切都是為了幾個牧羊人和一些羊，我的主啊？！」


"Really."

「真的。」


The Father had replied

父親已經回答了


with an amused,

帶著一個玩味的，


affectionate smile.

深情的微笑。


💠



One especially beautiful, delightfully bright angel --

一個特別美麗、令人愉悅的明亮的天使——


designed expressly for this unprecedented moment

專門為這個前所未有的時刻而設計


in all of human and Heavenly History --

在所有人類和天堂的歷史中——


was set to rise --

被設定為上升——


a brilliant new star, high in the sky --

一顆明亮的新星，高高在天上——


directly over the small, dark, ramshackle stable in Bethlehem

就在伯利恆的小而黑暗、破的馬廄上方


where Jesus would be born --

耶穌將在哪裡出生——


an unmistakable sign for stargazers

對於觀星者來說，這是一個明確無誤的訊號


of the momentous birth.

重大的誕生。


💠



Now the Father looked at Jesus expectantly.

現在，天父期待地看著耶穌。


Taking a deep breath, Jesus stood,

耶穌深吸一口氣，站著，


and bowed low

深深地鞠了一躬


before nestling into His Father's powerful, protective Arms

在依偎在他父親強大、保護的手臂之前


for a prolonged embrace.

為了長時間的擁抱。


He didn't want to leave Heaven

他不想離開天堂


but He would...

但他會......


out of Love.

因為愛。


💠



Based on Luke 2:1-14; Matthew 2:1-10; John 1:1-5,14,18; Luke 1:31-35; John 3:16.

基於路加福音2:1-14；馬太福音2:1-10；約翰福音1:1-5,14,18；路加福音1:31-35；約翰福音3:16。


💠

item.title: 2 - Messages 天使
item.description: The Father sends Gabriel to three people 父親把加百列送到了三個人那裡
item.content:encoded:
The Father looked,

父親眼，


and instantly, Gabriel,

立刻，加百列，


His Chief Messenger angel

他的首席信使天使


stood before the big Throne,

站在大寶座前，


respectful and alert.

尊重和願意。


"It's time for you to visit Zechariah the priest, in the Temple, Mary at home in Nazareth, and her fiancé, Joseph, the carpenter.

「是時候讓你去拜訪聖殿的祭司撒迦利亞，在拿撒勒的馬利亞，以及她的未婚夫，木匠約瑟夫。


"For the carpenter, be sure to visit in a dream,

「對於木匠來說，一定要在夢中拜訪，


not while he's awake.

不是在他醒著的時候。


It will be best for him that way."

那樣對他來說是最好的。」


Gabriel saluted smartly.

加百列巧妙地敬禮。


"Anything else, My Lord?"

「還有別的事嗎，大人？」


The Father smiled at His faithful servant.

父親對他忠實的僕人笑了笑。


"When you get back,

「當你回來的時候，


get the choir -- the big one -- ready!"

讓合唱團——大合唱團——做好準備！」


💠



Gabriel took care of his assignment quickly.

加百列很快就完成了他的任務。


Although time on Earth passes slowly --

儘管地球上的時間過得很慢——


a full six earth months separating the visits to Zechariah

從他第一次拜訪撒迦利亞


and Mary --

到第二次拜訪馬利亞，中間隔了整整六個月——


Gabriel, existing as he did in eternity,

加百列，就像他在永恆中一樣存在，


easily completed all three visits

輕鬆完成了所有三次訪問


in less than one heavenly day.

不到一個天堂一天。


💠



When he arrived back home in Heaven, the Chief Messenger angel stood before the great Throne to give his report.

當他回到天堂的家時，首席使者天使站在偉大的寶座前報告。


"How did things go?"

「事情怎麼樣了？」


"All Your messages have been delivered.

「你的所有資訊都已送達。


Mary has been to visit Elizabeth

馬利亞去拜訪了伊莉莎白


and both are aware of the other's pregnancy.

兩人都知道對方懷孕了。


And the carpenter and Mary have tied the knot, so to speak --

木匠和馬利亞結婚了——


not physically, of course."

當然，不是指物理意義上的。」


"And Zechariah?"

「撒迦利亞呢？」


Gabriel sighed, just a little, and tried not to roll his eyes.

加百列嘆了口氣，只是一點點，儘量不翻白眼。


"I had to strike him dumb for a little, to help him believe."

「我不得不暫時剝奪他的說話能力，幫助他相信這一點。」


💠



Based on Luke 1:5-79; Luke 2:13-14; John 1:6-9,15; Matthew 1:18-25; 2 Peter 3:8.

基於路加福音1:5-79；路加福音2:13-14；約翰福音1:6-9,15；馬太福音1:18-25；彼得後書3:8。


💠

item.title: 1 - Anticipation 預期
item.description: Jesus prepares Himself to become small 耶穌預備自己變得渺小
item.content:encoded:
The Father nudged Jesus

天父輕輕推了耶穌


as they sat together on their big Throne in Heaven.

當他們一起坐在天堂的大寶座上時。


"It's nearly

「這幾乎是


Time."

時間。」


💠



Jesus took a deep breath

耶穌深吸了一口氣


and shuddered a little

有點發抖


as He contemplated the strange transformation He was soon to undergo.

當他思考他即將經歷的奇怪轉變時。


He loved being in Heaven

他非常喜歡待在天堂


with His Father!

和他父親一起！


Together, They shared

他們一起分享了


unimaginable Beauty,

難以想象的美麗，


unlimited Power,

無限的力量，


unfathomable Wisdom,

最深邃的智慧，


unconquerable Love.

不可征服的愛。


Almost every moment was filled

幾乎每一刻都充滿了


with exquisite melodies of worship and adoration

帶著精緻的崇拜和崇拜的旋律


provided by luminous, harmonious, powerful angelic beings

由發光、和諧、強大的天使生物提供


eager to do their Lord's bidding.

渴望完成他們主的命令。


💠



How

怎麼


could He leave all this glory, beauty and holiness

他能拋棄所有的榮耀、美麗和聖潔嗎


to go down

下去


to a dirty, doubt-ridden, death-filled

到一個骯髒的、充滿懷疑的、充滿死亡的


world gone wrong --

世界出錯了——


not as the conquering Creator God He truly was,

祂並非以凱旋的造物主形像出現，但這才是祂的真實身分，


but as a tiny, helpless,

但作為一個渺小的、無助的，


impoverished,

貧窮的，


human

人類


infant?!

新生兒？！


💠



He knew what would happen to Him --

他知道他會發生什麼——


His entire life on earth

他在地球上的一生


from beginning to end --

從開始到結束——


for He and His Father had planned it

因為他和他父親已經計劃好了


together.

一起。


It would be the hardest thing He --

這將是他——


or anyone --

或者任何人——


had ever done.

經歷過的最艱難的事。


💠



Based on John 1:1-5,10-14,18; Matthew 1:18-25; Luke 1:30-35; Luke 2:34-35; Isaiah 53:2-12.

基於約翰福音1:1-5,10-14,18；馬太福音1:18-25；路加福音1:30-35；路加福音2:34-35；以賽亞書53:2-12。


💠

item.title: 0 - Prologue 序言
item.description: A story of Christmas 聖誕的故事
item.content:encoded:
Nearly everyone has heard the story of the birth of Jesus --

幾乎每個人都聽說過耶穌誕生的故事——


born of a virgin,

處女所生，


heralded by angels,

由天使宣布，


worshipped by shepherds,

被牧羊人崇拜，


receiving rich gifts from wise men from the east,

收到來自東方智者的豐富禮物，


His first home a stable,

他的第一個家是馬廄，


His first crib a feeding trough --

他的第一個嬰兒床是一個餵食槽——


or manger.

或者馬槽。


💠



But --

但是——


what was happening...

發生了什麼......


behind the scenes?

幕後？


💠



Based on Matthew 1:18-25; Matthew 2:1-18; Luke 1:5-80; Luke 2:1-39; John 1:1-18.

基於馬太福音1:18-25；馬太福音2:1-18；路加福音1:5-80；路加福音2:1-39；約翰福音1:1-18。


💠

item.title: 31 - Found 被找到
item.description: The Ruling Council learns the Apostles' whereabouts 執政理事會瞭解使徒的下落
item.content:encoded:
"Those men -- the ones you put in prison --

「那些人——你關進監獄的那些人——


they're standing in the Temple,

他們站在聖殿裡，


teaching the people!"

教導人民！」


Silence.

沉默。


The Chief Priest's servant wondered if the Council had heard him.

祭司長的僕人想知道理事會是否聽到了他的話。


He was about to repeat himself more loudly --

他正要更大聲地重複自己——


when the entire Council erupted in a hubbub of dismay.

當整個理事會爆發出沮喪的喧鬧聲時。


"What?!"

「什麼？！」


"That's impossible!"

「那是不可能的！」


"How?!"

「怎麼？！」


"Did someone release them?"

「有人釋放了他們嗎？」


"The guards must have fallen asleep!"

「警衛們肯定睡著了！」


And, most importantly:

而且，最重要的是：


"Now, what?"

「現在，什麼？」


💠



The truth was, for all their show

事實是，儘管他們表面上如此裝腔作勢


of authority and power,

權威和權力，


the Chief Priest and the entire Ruling Council were deathly afraid

祭司長和整個統治理事會都害怕得要死


of the people,

人民的，


who were following the new movement like dimwitted sheep.

他們像一隻笨羊一樣跟隨著新運動。


The rulers were well aware that the Galilean rabbi -- whom they had condemned to death -- had been revered by many as a prophet from God.

統治者們很清楚，他們判處死刑的那位加利利教員，被許多人視為上帝的先知。


And now the rabbi's followers were rising in popularity --

現在那教員的追隨者越來越受歡迎——


especially

尤其


with the recent, very public healing

隨著最近的、非常公開的奇蹟般的治癒


of the lame man -- Azariah -- not long ago.

不久前，那個瘸子——阿札裡亞。


It was fine to arrest such deceivers under cover of darkness...

在黑暗中逮捕這樣的騙子是可以的......


but a wholly different matter to arrest them in broad daylight,

但在光天化日之下逮捕他們是完全不同的事情，


before the very eyes of the people

在人們的眼前


who held them in such high esteem.

誰如此尊重他們。


💠



At last, not willing to appear as fools or cowards before the Temple guards and servants,

最後，不願意在聖殿守衛和僕人面前表現為傻瓜或懦夫，


the entire Ruling Council rose, together with their attendants,

整個統治理事會及其隨從都站了起來，


and headed toward the Temple en masse,

並集體走向聖殿，


hoping against hope

希望


the people would hesitate

人們會猶豫不決


to stone

用石刑


such a large group.

在對付這麼大的群。


💠



Based on Acts 3:1-10; 5:25-26.

基於使徒行傳3:1-10；5:25-26。


💠

item.title: 30 - Missing 失蹤
item.description: The guard reports to the Council 警衛向理事會報告
item.content:encoded:
Full of curiosity, the Chief Priest frowned, then gestured to the guard, who came forward and saluted respectfully.

祭司長充滿了好奇心，皺起了眉頭，然後向警衛示意，警衛上前恭敬地敬禮。


"Please give your report to the Council."

「請向理事會說你的報告。」


The guard spoke nervously.

警衛緊張地說話。


"We went to the prison and found it carefully locked up, and the guards standing before bolted doors;

「我們去了監獄，發現它被小心翼翼地鎖上了，警衛們站在鎖著的門前；


but when we opened them --" he gulped,

但當我們打開門時——」他焦急地說，


"there was no one inside!"

「裡面沒有人！」


💠



"What?!"

「什麼？！」


Caiaphas gave the guard a hard stare.

該亞法狠狠地盯著警衛。


"No one there!?"

「那裡沒有人！？」


A brief pause... then, more menacingly:

短暫的停頓......然後，更具有威脅性：


"Are you certain?"

「你確定嗎？」


"Yes, Your Excellency."

「是的，閣下。」


The guard bowed his head, feeling somehow as though the bad news was his fault.

警衛低下了頭，總覺得壞訊息是他的錯。


Now the entire Council chamber fell silent, as all pondered in perplexity.

現在，整個理事會廳都沉默了，所有人都在困惑中沉思。


What could have happened

究竟遭遇了什麼


to the false Messiah's followers?

那些假彌賽亞的追隨者們？


💠



Just then, one of Caiaphas' household servants entered,

就在這時，該亞法的一個僕人進來了，


went straight to the Chief Priest,

直接去找祭司長，


and whispered in his ear.

在他耳邊低語。


Caiaphas threw up his hands, then gestured toward the Council.

該亞法舉起雙手，然後向理事會做了個手勢。


"Tell them," he said

「告訴他們，」他說


with resignation.

無奈地。


💠



Based on Acts 5:22-25.

基於使徒行傳5:22-25。


💠

item.title: 29 - Freed 釋放的
item.description: The Apostles are not found 使徒們不見了
item.content:encoded:
John looked around.

約翰環顧四周。


The angel had disappeared, yet he -- and all the Apostles -- were still standing together in the darkened street!

天使已經消失了，但他——和所有使徒——仍然一起站在黑暗的街道上！


They stared at one another.

他們互相凝視著。


Were they dreaming?

他們在做夢嗎？


Or were they really free?

還是他們真的是自由的？


Either way, they had better obey.

無論哪種方式，他們最好服從。


They headed off toward the Temple

他們向聖殿走去


as the sky began to lighten in the east.

當東方的天空開始變亮時。


💠



Caiaphas surveyed the Council Hall with satisfaction.

該亞法滿意地觀了議會大廳。


All were present and accounted for -- not hastily, but at his leisure.

所有人都到齊了，人數都清點完畢──不是匆忙，而是在他有條不紊的情況下。


The Chief Priest had enjoyed the respite afforded by keeping the false Messiah's main followers in jail.

大祭司透過將假彌賽亞的主要追隨者關在監獄裡而享受了喘息。


However, it was time to deal with them sternly,

然而，是時候嚴厲對待他們了，


and end this atrocious movement,

結束這場可怕的運動，


once and for all.

一勞永逸。


He turned to the bailiff.

他轉向法庭監守。


"Bring in the accused."

「把被告們帶進來。」


💠



The bailiff nodded to the guards, who saluted smartly and exited the Council Hall.

法庭監守向警衛點了點頭，衛兵們敬了個漂亮的軍禮，然後離開了議會大廳。


All waited patiently, some chatting quietly to their neighbour, shifting in their seats, arranging long robes.

所有人都耐心地等待著，有些人靜靜地和鄰居聊天，在座位上挪動一下，整理長袍。


Hmmm...

誒......


The wait seemed to be taking longer than usual...!

等待的時間似乎比平時要長......！


💠



At last, the guards returned emptyhanded, and one of them went straight to the bailiff and whispered in his ear.

最後，警衛們空手而歸，其中一個直接走到法庭監守那裡，在他耳邊低聲說。


The bailiff's eyes widened.

法庭監守睜大了眼睛。


"Are you sure?!"

「你確定嗎？！」


💠



Based on Acts 5:21.

基於使徒行傳5:21。


💠

item.title: 28 - Jailed again 再次入獄
item.description: The Apostles are arrested 使徒們被捕了
item.content:encoded:
"Open up in there!"

「打開這扇門！」


Someone was pounding at the door.

有人在猛烈地敲門。


John got up sleepily and, without thinking, opened the door

約翰昏昏欲睡地起床，不假思索地開啟了門


to a sudden, chaotic invasion of torches, leather, weapons --

火炬、皮革、武器的突然、混亂的入侵——


and rough treatment.

和動粗。


When the dust had settled, all of Jesus' chosen Apostles were in jail --

當塵埃落定時，耶穌選擇的所有使徒都在監獄裡——


with armed guards standing before doors

武裝警衛站在門前


firmly bolted from the outside.

從外面牢牢地栓住。


💠



Caiaphas took his time calling together the Ruling Council.

該亞法並沒有急於統召集治理事會議。


There was no rush, now that the main followers of the false Messiah were safely ensconced away from their adoring -- he grimaced -- public.

既然假彌賽亞的主要追隨者已被安全地監禁起來，遠離了崇拜他們的公眾——他做了個鬼臉——那就不用著急了。


Let them stew in jail!

讓他們待在監獄裡！


The Council would be convened when he was good and ready.

理事會將在他準備好並準備好時召開。


💠



John ate a little of the bread the two Marys had brought to the jail.

約翰吃了一點兩位馬利亞帶到監獄的麵包。


Although he could fast, he appreciated their loving gesture to support the Apostles during their imprisonment.

雖然他可以禁食，但他很欣賞他們在使徒被監禁期間支援他們的慈愛姿態。


He'd overheard the latest guards saying the Council was to be convened in the morning.

他無意中聽到新來的衛兵說，事會將於明天早上召開。


The circumstances of their imprisonment had not been pleasant,

他們被監禁的情況並不愉快，


but he trusted the Holy Spirit would give them yet another opportunity

但他相信聖靈會再給他們一次機會


to testify boldly to Jesus' resurrection.

大膽地見證耶穌的復活。


💠



The cell door creaked open.

牢房的門吱呀一聲開了。


John sat up drowsily.

約翰昏昏欲睡地坐了起來。


It was still dark... not time for their hearing yet...?

天還黑......現在就進行審判還為時過早......？


A bright light entered the cell, and the Apostles shielded sleep-infused eyes.

明亮的光線進入牢房，使徒們遮蔽了充滿睡眠的眼睛。


"Hurry, get up!" commanded the luminous being.

「快點，起來！」 指揮發光的存在。


Confused yet obedient, many imagining they were merely dreaming,

困惑但聽話，許多人想象他們只是在做夢，


they followed the angel out the door,

他們跟著天使出了門，


around the guards --

在警衛周圍——


who didn't seem to notice them! --

誰似乎沒有注意到他們！--


and outside into the street.

然後走到外面，到街上去。


"Go," said the angel to them all.

「去吧，」天使對他們所有人說。


"Stand in the Temple, and speak to the people

「站在聖殿裡，向人們講話


all these words of life."

所有這些生命之言。」


💠



Based on Acts 5:17-20.

基於使徒行傳5:17-20。


💠

item.title: 27 - Peter's popularity 彼得的受歡迎程度
item.description: Caiaphas is indignant 該亞法很憤慨
item.content:encoded:
"He's coming!"

「他來！」


Caiaphas was pleased the crowd was excited to see him, their Chief Priest, as,

該亞法很高興看到群眾興奮地見到他，他們的大祭司，因為，


on his way to the Temple,

在去聖殿的路上，


he walked the streets of Jerusalem.

他走在耶路撒冷的街道上。


But... what was this?!

但是......這是什麼？！


People were carrying the sick out on mats, right into the street...!

人們把許多病人放在墊子上抬到街上……！


He turned and looked behind him...

他轉過身來，看了看身後......


only to see

然後他看到


one of the false Messiah's more prominent followers --

假彌賽亞更突出的追隨者之一——


an unschooled fisherman named Peter,

一個名叫彼得的未上學的漁夫，


whom the Ruling Council had recently arrested, warned --

統治委員會最近逮捕了誰，警告說——


and magnanimously released! --

並慷慨他釋放！--


walking a distance behind him,

在他身後走一段距離，


apparently heading, as he was, to the Temple.

顯然，正如他一樣，前往聖殿。


💠



All of the sick people were placed on the side of the street opposite the sun

所有的病人都被放在太陽對面的街道邊上


so that, as he passed, Peter's shadow might fall upon them.

這樣，當他經過時，彼得的影子可能會落在他們身上。


Every person on whom Peter's shadow fell --

如果彼得的影子落在某人身上——


got up -- some quickly, some more cautiously -- but all...

他們起床了——有些很快，有些更謹慎——但所有......


fully healed!?

完全治癒了！？


They rolled up their mats and followed Peter --

他們捲起墊子，跟著彼得——


dancing and leaping and shouting for joy!

跳舞、跳躍、歡呼！


💠



Peter saw the Chief Priest ahead, staring with furrowed brow.

彼得看到前面的大祭司正皺著眉頭看著他。


"Your Excellency," he nodded respectfully as he passed.

「閣下，」他經過時恭敬地點了點頭。


The Chief Priest flushed and clenched his fists.

大祭司臉紅了，握緊了拳頭。


How dare the illiterate commoner mock him!

那個目不識丁的平民竟敢嘲笑他！


He pondered for a moment.

他沉思了一會兒。


This fledgling movement threatened to up-end everything they had worked so hard to accomplish.

這場剛剛起步的運動有可能使他們努力完成的一切結束。


It was time to take further steps.

是時候採取進一步措施了。


💠



Based on Acts 5:12-17.

基於使徒行傳5:12-17。


💠

item.title: 26 - Refill 再裝滿
item.description: Peter, John and Azariah are released 彼得、約翰和亞撒利雅被釋放
item.content:encoded:
As they headed back to the house where they were staying with the other Apostles in Jerusalem,

當他們回到他們和耶路撒冷其他使徒一起住的房子時，


Peter and John had no doubt

彼得和約翰毫不懷疑


the religious authorities would make good on their threats,

宗教當局將兌現他們的威脅，


if they could.

如果他們可以的話。


The awful vision of their beloved Master...

他們心愛的主人的痛苦願景......


cruelly humiliated, tortured and crucified...

殘酷的羞辱、折磨和被釘在十字架上......


in accordance with God's predetermined Will...

根據上帝預定的旨意......


was forever etched in their memories.

永遠銘刻在他們的記憶中。


Both Apostles were fully aware that one day, in the future,

兩位使徒都充分意識到，有一天，在未來，


when it was their turn,

輪到他們的時候，


they would suffer a similar fate.

他們會遭受類似的命運。


Their Lord had already told them so.

他們的主已經告訴他們了。


💠



Azariah headed home, too, to rejoin his family,

亞撒利雅也回家了，與家人團聚，


who still struggled to comprehend

他們仍在努力理解


the no-longer-lame man's miraculous healing.

不再腿瘸的子的人的奇蹟般的治癒。


He looked forward to contributing to the family finances

他期待為家庭財務做出貢獻


through honest, able-bodied labour -- for the very first time.

透過誠實、身體有力的勞動——這可是他一生中第一次啊。


Someday, he imagined, he might join the Apostles in their amazing work.

他想象著，有一天，他可能會加入使徒們的驚人工作。


Until then, he would contribute in the way he'd always longed to, but never could.

在那之前，他會以他一直渴望但從未實現的方式做出貢獻。


The resurrected Messiah had given him his heart's desire.

復活的彌賽亞給了他內心的渴望。


He would never beg again.

他再也不會乞求了。


💠



The Holy Spirit was pleased to see that, upon hearing Peter and John's report,

聖靈很高興看到，在聽到彼得和約翰的報告後，


the Apostles and disciples at the house in Jerusalem

耶路撒冷房子裡的使徒和門徒


chose to respond with undivided prayer --

選擇以團結的祈禱來回應——


not for protection from the Council's threats --

不是為了保護免受理事會的威脅——


but for boldness

但要獲得勇氣


to speak God's words in the face of danger,

面對危險說出上帝的話，


and for healing and miracles following

以及治癒和奇蹟跟隨


in the Name of Jesus,

以耶穌的名義，


to confirm them.

驗證它們。


💠



When they had finished praying, the Holy Spirit

當他們祈禱完後，聖靈


gladly

欣然


shook the whole place

震動了整個地方


and refilled them all

並把他們全部重新填充


with power and boldness

帶著奇蹟的力量和大膽


again...

再一次...


and again...

再一次......


and again...

再一次......


and again.

再一次。


💠



Based on John 21:17-19; Matthew 20:20-23; Acts 4:21-31.

基於約翰福音21:17-19；馬太福音20:20-23；使徒行傳4:21-31。


💠

item.title: 25 - Before the Council 4 站在理事會之前 4
item.description: The Ruling Council comes to a decision 執政委員會做出了決定
item.content:encoded:
As the Ruling Council began their deliberations in private, Caiaphas spread his hands toward the others.

當執政委員會開始私下審議時，該亞法向其他人張開雙手。


"What can we do with these men?"

「我們能對這些人做什麼？」


Annas, the previous year's Chief Priest -- and Caiaphas' father-in-law -- replied immediately: "The important thing is to silence them. This word and this Name must spread no further among the people!"

亞那，前一年的大祭司——也是該亞法的嶽父——立即回答說：「重要的是讓他們閉嘴。 這個詞和這個名字不能在人民中進一步傳播！」


💠



Still ashamed of his own silence

仍然為自己的沉默感到羞愧


in the shadow of Jesus' crucifixion,

在耶穌被釘十字架的陰影下，


Nicodemus -- who had once visited Jesus at night -- stood.

尼哥底母——他曾經在晚上拜訪過耶穌——站了起來。


"We cannot punish these men for healing a lame man. Everyone in Jerusalem is praising God for this!"

「我們不能懲罰這些人治癒一個瘸子的人。 耶路撒冷的每個人都在為此讚美上帝！」


"It is an outstanding miracle," agreed Joseph of Arimathea. He had placed Jesus' body in his own, newly constructed tomb.

「這是一個傑出的奇蹟，」阿里馬太的約瑟夫同意了。 他之前曾將耶穌的遺體安放在他新建的墳墓裡。


"The man who was healed is over forty years old!"

「那個被治癒的人已經四十多歲了！」


Others in the Council nodded, murmuring in agreement.

理事會中的其他人點了點頭，喃喃自語表示同意。


Caiaphas pondered, then spoke heavily.

該亞法沉思著，然後沉重地說話。


"We must forbid them, then."

「那麼，我們必須禁止他們。」


💠



When the three men were once again standing before the Council,

當這三個人再次站在理事會面前時，


Caiaphas cleared his throat.

該亞法清了清嗓子。


"We, the Ruling Council of Jerusalem -- who sit in Moses' seat! --

「我們，耶路撒冷執政委員會——坐在摩西的座位上！——」


he reminded them severely,

他嚴厲地提醒他們，


"charge you

「向你收費


not to speak or teach at all

根本不說話或教


in the name of the false Messiah:" --

以假彌賽亞的名義：」--


he gritted his teeth as he spoke the hated name --

他咬牙切齒地說著討厭的名字——


"Jesus of Nazareth!"

「拿撒勒的耶穌！」


He paused for effect, then continued forcefully.

他停頓了一下，然後繼續強調地說。


"Should you fail to adhere to this restriction,

「如果你不遵守這個限制，


we will find it necessary to take further steps."

我們會發現有必要採取進一步的步驟。」


He left the "further steps" to the men's imaginations,

他把「進一步的步驟」留給了男人的想象，


trusting they would be sufficiently intimidated.

相信他們會受到充分的恐嚇。


💠



Instantly, the wind of the Holy Spirit rose and spoke confidently, yet kindly, through Peter:

聖靈的風立刻升起，透過彼得自信而親切地說話：


"You must decide for yourselves

「你們必須自己決定


whether it is right in God's sight

在上帝看來它是否正確


to obey you --

服從你——


rather than God."

而不是上帝。」


Overflowing with Holy Spirit joy, John chimed in.

充滿聖靈的喜悅，約翰插了進來。


"We can't help talking about the things we've seen and heard!"

「我們忍不住談論我們所見所聞！」


Caiaphas was alarmed.

該亞法驚慌失措。


The two men seemed about to launch into enthusiastic dialogue.

兩人似乎即將展開熱烈的對話。


He signalled the Temple guards quickly.

他迅速向聖殿守衛示意。


"Release them!"

「放開他們！」


💠



Based on Acts 4:15-22; John 3:1-12; John 7:45-52; Matthew 27:57-60; Mark 15:43-46; Luke 23:50-53; John 19:38-42.

基於使徒行傳4:15-22；約翰福音3:1-12；約翰福音7:45-52；馬太福音27:57-60；馬可福音15:43-46；路加福音23:50-53；約翰福音19:38-42。


💠

item.title: 24 - Before the Council 3 站在理事會之前 3
item.description: Peter and John complete the Holy Spirit's remarks about Jesus 彼得同約翰完成咗聖靈關於耶穌嘅話
item.content:encoded:
Caiaphas shuddered at the awful memory,

該亞法對這可怕的記憶感到不寒而慄，


pulled himself together,

鎮定下來，


and returned his attention to the three men standing before the Council,

並把注意力回到站在理事會面前的三個人身上，


as the Holy Spirit concluded, through John, His remarks about Jesus:

正如聖靈透過約翰完成了他對耶穌的評論：


"Salvation cannot be found in anyone else,

「救贖不能在其他人身上找到，


for there is no other name under Heaven

因為天堂下沒有其他名字


given to mankind,

給人類，


by which we must be saved."

我們必須因此得救。」


💠



One of the three standing before the Council was well known to all of them:

站在理事會面前的三個人中，有一個人是理事會裡大家都很熟悉的：


Azariah, the man crippled from birth, who had formerly sat every day

亞撒利雅，這個從出生起就殘廢的人，以前每天都坐著


at the Temple gate begging --

在聖殿門口乞討——


but now -- undeniably, miraculously healed.

但現在——不可否認，奇蹟般地治癒了。


The other two were clearly common men who had never learned to read or write --

另外兩個人顯然是窮人，從未學過讀書寫字——


some of the Galilean rabbi's ragtag followers, apparently!

顯然，那個加利利教員的一些雜七雜八的追隨者！


Yet here they were, in an unthinkable fusion of miracle, courage and eloquence.

然而，他們在這裡，在神跡、勇氣和口才的不可思議的融合中。


💠



Caiaphas shook his head.

該亞法搖了搖頭。


What to do?

怎麼辦？


The other members of the Ruling Council were silent.

執政理事會的其他成員保持沉默。


What could they say?

他們能說什麼？


After a long pause, the Chief Priest signalled the Temple guards.

停頓了很久之後，大祭司向聖殿守衛手勢。


"Remove them."

「把他們帶出房間。」


💠



Based on Acts 4:5-7,12-15.

基於使徒行傳4:5-7,12-15。


💠

item.title: 23 - Before the Council 2 站在理事會之前 2
item.description: John speaks, Caiaphas remembers 約翰說話，該亞法記得
item.content:encoded:
When the council hall grew quiet again, the Holy Spirit spoke clearly and decisively through John:

當議會大廳再次安靜下來時，聖靈透過約翰清晰而果斷地說話：


"He is the stone you builders rejected:

「他是你們建造者拒絕的石頭：


He is now the chief cornerstone."

他現在是首席基石。」


Caiaphas' heart dropped

該亞法的心摔了


into his stomach,

進入他的胃裡，


as the Chief Priest was instantly, unwillingly transported back to that day:

當大祭司立刻不由自主地回想起了那一天的記憶：


the day they had challenged the upstart Galilean rabbi

那天，他們向那個惹是生非的加利利教員挑戰


in the Temple.

在聖殿裡。


💠



In addition to refusing to answer

除了拒絕回答之外


the perfectly legitimate question (which they had just asked the two men),

完全合理的問題（他們剛剛問過這兩個人），


the false Messiah had then told a story to the crowd --

假彌賽亞隨後向眾人講了一個故事——


against them, the Ruling Council! -- portraying them as rogue tenant farmers in God's vineyard,

反對他們，執政理事會！——把他們描繪成上帝葡萄園裡的流氓佃農，


who attacked and beat His servants, and even

誰攻擊並毆打他的僕人，甚至


killed

謀殺的


His own Son!

他自己的兒子！


Caiaphas' face flushed

該亞法的臉漲得通紅


with remembered outrage.

帶著記憶中的憤怒。


💠



Then the rabbi had turned and spoken directly to him.

然後教員轉向他並直接對他說話。


"Because of this, I tell you:

「正因為如此，我告訴你：


the Kingdom of God will be taken from you,

神的國度將從你手中奪走，


and given to a people producing its fruit."

並給予一個結出果實的民族。」


"No... never...!" Caiaphas had gasped.

「不......從來沒有......！」 該亞法喘著粗氣。


💠



Jesus had looked at him intently.

耶穌專注地看著他。


"Have you never read in the Holy Writings:

「你從未讀過《聖書》嗎：


'The stone the builders rejected

「建築商拒絕的石頭


has become the chief cornerstone;

已經成為主要的基石；


this is from the LORD, and it is wonderful in our eyes'?"

這是來自耶和華的，在我們眼中是奇妙的'？」


He paused for a moment, then added gently:

他停頓了一會兒，然後溫和地補充道：


"And whoever falls on this stone

「誰掉在這塊石頭上


will be broken,

將會破碎，


and the one on whom it falls

以及它落在身上的人


will be crushed."

將會被壓垮。」


💠



Based on Acts 4:5-7,11; Luke 20:1-19; Psalms 118:22-23.

基於使徒行傳4:5-7,11；路加福音20:1-19；詩篇118:22-23。


💠

item.title: 22 - Before the Council 1 站在理事會之前 1
item.description: Peter and John are questioned by the Chief Priest 彼得和約翰被大祭司審問
item.content:encoded:
The next morning the two unschooled fishermen were reunited with Azariah,

第二天早上，兩個沒有受過教育的漁民與亞撒利雅團聚了，


the no-longer-lame, former beggar

不再跛腳，前乞丐


healed by the Holy Spirit.

被聖靈治癒。


The three men stood quietly before a hastily convened hearing

這三個人靜靜地站在匆忙召開的聽證會前


of the highly educated, religious elite: the Jewish Ruling Council.

受過高等教育的宗教精英：猶太統治理事會。


As Chief Priest, Caiaphas took the lead:

身為大祭司，該亞法開始審問：


"In what power or name did you do this?"

「你以什麼權力或名義這樣做的？」


💠



In response, the wind of the Holy Spirit rose and spoke confidently through Peter:

作為回應，聖靈的風升起，透過彼得自信地說話：


"Rulers of the people, elders of Israel:

「人民的統治者，以色列的長老們：


if we are being called to account today for an act of kindness shown to a lame man --

如果我們今天因對一個瘸子表現出的善意行為而被追究責任——


how he was healed --

他是如何被治癒的——


then know this, you, and all the people of Israel:

那麼，你和以色列全體人民都應該知道這——點：


In the name of Jesus the Messiah, from Nazareth --

以彌賽亞耶穌的名義，來自拿撒勒——


whom you crucified! --

你們把他釘在十字架上！ ——


and whom God has raised from the dead! --

而神使他從死裡復活！ ——


in Him this man stands before you healed!"

在他裡面，站在你面前的人已經治癒了！ 」


💠



In the midst of the ensuing uproar, Caiaphas clenched his fists.

在隨後的動盪中，該亞法握緊了拳頭。


This common labourer had just spoken the last words

這個普通的勞工剛剛說出了的話


the Chief Priest had ever wanted to hear.

大祭司長最不想聽到。


At that moment, a trusted servant from his household approached and whispered in his ear.

那一刻，他家裡一個值得信賴的僕人走過來，在他耳邊低聲說。


"These men were with Him -- with Jesus."

「這些人已經花了很多時間與他——與耶穌在一起。」


💠



Based on Acts 4:5-10,13.

基於使徒行傳4:5-10，13。


💠

item.title: 21 - Jailed 入獄的
item.description: Peter and John are arrested 彼得和約翰被捕
item.content:encoded:
That night, Peter and John rejoiced together

那天晚上，彼得和約翰一起歡喜


over the Holy Spirit's healing

關於聖靈的治癒


of the lame man at the Temple --

聖殿裡那個瘸子的人——


in the name of Jesus!

以耶穌的名義！


This had led to another amazing opportunity to let everyone know:

這帶來了另一個讓大家知道的絕佳機會：


the Son of God,

上帝的兒子，


the holy and righteous Prince of Life --

神聖而正義的生命王子——


whom they had condemned to death! --

他們判處誰死刑！——


had risen from the dead.

從死裡復活了。


💠



Of course, the religious authorities,

當然，宗教權威，


the instigators behind Jesus' execution,

耶穌處決背後的煽動者，


had not taken kindly to the Holy Spirit's words.

不喜歡聖靈的話語。


As soon as they'd realized what was happening,

他們一意識到發生了什麼，


they'd arrested the two Apostles

他們逮捕了兩個使徒


and thrown them in jail...

把他們扔進監獄......


but not before

但不是之前


five thousand listeners

五千名聽眾


had come to faith!

已經信仰了！


💠



Caiaphas grimaced. The Chief Priest had been very confident in his victory over the upstart Galilean rabbi.

該亞法愁眉苦臉。大祭司非常有自信，他已經打敗了挑戰他權威的加利利教員。


How could he not, with his enemy humiliated, exposed, tortured

他怎麼可能不自信呢當那個敵人已經被羞辱、被揭露、被折磨時，


and crucified --

並被釘在十字架上——


for all the world to see?

讓全世界都能看到？


When the false Messiah was dead and buried --

當假彌賽亞死亡並被埋葬時——


His followers demoralized --

他的追隨者士氣低落——


Caiaphas had celebrated...

該亞法慶祝了......


breathed a sigh of relief...

鬆了一口氣......


and moved on.

並繼續前進。


So --

所以——


what

為什麼


was this throbbing headache?

劇烈的頭痛嗎？


And who

誰


were these two men?!

是這兩個人嗎？！


💠



Based on Acts 3:1-26; Acts 4:1-4.

基於使徒行傳3:1-26；使徒行傳4:1-4。


💠

item.title: 20 - First steps 最初的行走步驟
item.description: The healed beggar learns to walk 奇蹟般治癒的乞丐試圖行走
item.content:encoded:
Azariah took a cautious first step...

亞撒利雅邁出了謹慎的第一步......


then another...

然後是另一個...


and another --

還有另一個——


with increasing boldness! --

越來越大膽！--


as Peter, John and the Holy Spirit looked on with delight.

當彼得、約翰和聖靈喜悅地看著他時。


Soon, the no-longer-lame man

很快，這個不再瘸腿的人


couldn't stop moving!

無法停止走動！


With further experimentation, he figured out how to jump --

透過進一步的實驗，他弄清楚瞭如何跳躍——


and even land -- safely! --

甚至落地——安全！——


to the great astonishment of all those standing by.

令所有旁觀者大為驚訝。


💠



People came running as Azariah began leaping ecstatically and crying out for joy.

當阿札裡亞開始欣喜若狂地跳躍並歡呼時，人們紛紛跑過來。


Everyone knew who he was:

每個人都知道他是誰：


the crippled beggar had been a fixture

殘疾乞丐一直在那裡


at the gate of the Temple

在聖殿的門口


for as long as anyone could remember.

只要任何人都能記得。


Now he was hanging on to the Apostles --

現在他緊緊抓住使徒們——


now following them inside --

現在跟著他們進去——


laughing, crying, leaping, shouting praise to God --

笑、哭、跳躍、高呼讚美上帝——


and generally creating

並且創造


an exuberant ruckus.

歡樂的騷動。


💠



As they walked along, Peter and John exchanged grateful grins.

彼得和約翰一邊走一邊互相交換感激的笑容。


This Holy Spirit certainly knew

這個聖靈肯定知道


how to amplify Jesus

如何放大耶穌


in the most amazing way.

以最驚人的方式。


💠



Based on Acts 3:1-11.

基於使徒行傳3:1-11。


💠

item.title: 19 - Hope reborn 希望重生
item.description: The lame man responds to the Holy Spirit 瘸子對聖靈的回應
item.content:encoded:
"Rise up...

「站起來......


and walk!"

並且走路！」


As the lame man stared at the Apostle's outstretched hand,

當瘸子盯著使徒伸出的手時，


hope,

希望，


reborn,

重生，


overflowed his heart...

填滿了他的心，以至於它溢位來......


dispelling every doubt.

消除每一個疑慮。


💠



With surreal faith, the beggar

帶著超現實的信仰，那個乞丐


seized Peter's hand

抓住彼得的手


and pulled himself up to standing --

並把自己拉起來站著——


for the very first time! --

第一次！--


his misshapen feet and ankles following suit! --

他七扭八歪的腳和腳踝也跟著！--


unthinkably,

難以想象的，


miraculously,

奇蹟般地，


obediently

順從地


becoming healthy and strong.

變得健康和強壯。


💠



He had never stood before -- except in dreams.

他以前從未站過——除了在夢中。


But now he was.

但現在他是。


Standing!

站著！


The former lame man stared at his perfectly formed feet and ankles --

這位前瘸子盯著他完美的腳和腳踝——


they were so beautiful!

他們太漂亮了！


He pinched himself, then slapped his face a few times, as Peter and John looked on with wide grins.

他掐了自己一下，然後又打了自己幾巴掌，而彼得和約翰則咧嘴大笑著看著。


At last, Azariah was certain:

最後，亞撒利雅確信了：


This...

這個...


was real.

是真的。


💠



Based on Acts 3:6-8.

基於使徒行傳3:6-8。


💠

item.title: 18 - Good eyes 好眼睛
item.description: What the beggar saw 乞丐看到了什麼
item.content:encoded:
Azariah was startled by what he saw

亞撒利亞對他所見的景象感到驚訝


when he finally dared look at the two fellows standing before him:

當他終於敢於看著站在他面前的兩個傢伙時：


good eyes...

好眼睛......


clear, patient, kindly eyes...

清澈、耐心、親切的眼睛......


joy-filled eyes...

充滿喜悅的眼睛......


looking back at him.

回頭看他。


Perhaps they

也許他們


might give more than most!

可能會給比大多數人更多！


💠



"I have no money -- no silver or gold --" began Peter.

「我沒有錢——沒有銀子或金子——」彼得開始說。


The beggar's face fell.

乞丐的臉色變得悲傷起來。


"But what i do have, i give you."

「但我確實擁有的，我會給你。」


Azariah looked up hopefully.

亞撒利亞希望地抬頭看了看。


Perhaps it was something small -- the two men didn't appear to be carrying anything...

也許是小東西——這兩個人似乎沒有攜帶任何東西......


💠



"In the name of Jesus Christ of Nazareth --"

「以拿撒勒的耶穌基督的名義——」


The lame man's heart leapt

瘸子的心跳加快了


at the sound of that name...

當他聽到這個名字時......


the name in which he had once hoped!

這個名字曾經讓他充滿希望！


Could it be...?!

會是...？！


His heart pounded wildly.

他的心臟劇烈地怦怦直跳。


But... the rabbi -- Jesus -- was dead... and gone. Everyone knew it.

但是......教員——耶穌——死了......並且走了。 每個人都知道。


So...

所以...


who --

誰——


were

是


these?!

這些人？！


💠



Based on Acts 3:1-6.

基於使徒行傳3:1-6。


💠

item.title: 17 - "Look at us." 「看看我們。」
item.description: Peter and John speak to the lame man 彼得和約翰與瘸腿的人說話
item.content:encoded:
Peter stared at the poor beggar's outstretched hand, then at the man himself.

彼得盯著這個可憐的乞丐伸出的手，然後盯著那個男人自己。


It was clear he could not walk -- his feet and ankles shrivelled and misshapen.

很明顯，他不能走路——他的腳和腳踝萎縮和失形。


The Apostle fished in his pocket, but came up empty. He looked at John, who shook his head and spread his hands a little.

使徒檢查了口袋，什麼也沒找到。他看了看約翰，約翰搖了搖頭，微微攤開雙手。


Both were broke.

兩人都沒有錢。


💠



Looking at the man's face, Peter saw downcast eyes.

彼得看著那人的臉，發現他的眼睛正低著。


A profound compassion rising up in his belly, the Apostle opened his mouth to speak:

深深的同情在他的肚子裡升起，使徒開口說：


"Look at us."

「看看我們。」


The words came gently, effortlessly, and Peter and John both knew:

這句話說慈，毫不費力，彼得和約翰都知道：


the wind of the Holy Spirit was picking up.

聖靈之風開始吹拂。


💠



Azariah was startled.

阿撒利亞大吃一驚。


Look at them?!

看看他們？！


Most people just tossed him a careless coin or two and continued on their way --

大多數人只是粗心大意地向他扔了一兩枚硬幣，然後繼續前行——


barely noticing the lame man.

幾乎沒有注意到那個瘸子。


What could these strangers want from him?

這些陌生人能從他那裡得到什麼？


💠



Slowly, fearfully, the crippled beggar raised shame-filled eyes,

慢慢地，害怕地，殘廢的乞丐抬起了充滿羞恥的眼睛，


as Peter and John waited patiently... prayerfully...

當彼得和約翰耐心地等待時......祈禱著......


both joyfully aware:

兩人都欣喜地意識到：


the Holy Spirit was about to do...

聖靈即將做......


something wonderful.

太棒了的某事。


💠



Based on John 3:8; Acts 3:1-5.

基於約翰福音3:8；使徒行傳3:1-5。


💠

item.title: 16 - The lame man 瘸子
item.description: Azariah begs outside the Temple 亞撒利雅在聖殿外乞求
item.content:encoded:
Azariah sighed.

亞撒利雅嘆了口氣。


His father and brother were carrying him to the gate of the Temple, as they did every day,

他的父親和哥哥像往常一樣把他抬到聖殿門口，


so he could help support the family the only way he was able:

所以他可以用他唯一能的方式幫助養家餬口：


by begging.

透過乞討。


💠



The hope was, and at times it was so, that those better off would be in a generous mood --

希望是，有時也是如此，那些過得更好的人會心情很慷慨——


or at least inclined to do something to please God --

或者至少傾向於做一些事情來取悅上帝——


when going up to the Temple to pray.

去聖殿祈禱的時候。


Azariah shook his head.

亞撒利雅搖了搖頭。


Begging was demeaning.

乞求是有辱人格的。


He yearned for a less humiliating way to help, but --

他渴望一種不那麼丟臉的幫助方式，但是——


there were not many options for a man

選擇並不多


crippled from birth.

對於一個天生無法行走的人來說。


💠



These past three years had been exciting, with stories of miracles, healings, signs, wonders -- food multiplied -- even the dead raised! --

過去三年是令人興奮的，有奇蹟、治癒、跡象、奇蹟的故事——食物成倍增加——甚至死者復活了！——


all through a poor

所有這些奇蹟透過一個貧窮的


carpenter-turned-rabbi

木匠轉為教員


from Nazareth in Galilee.

來自加利利的拿撒勒。


Azariah was most captivated, however,

然而，亞撒利雅最著迷了，


by stories of lame folks --

透過跛腳人的故事——


walking!

走路！


The reluctant beggar had desperately hoped

這個不情願的乞丐拼命地希望


he himself

他本人


might become one of those

可能會成為其中之一


miraculously healed...

奇蹟般地治癒了人 ......


but -- despite his most fervent prayers --

但是——儘管他最熱切地祈禱——


he and the miracle-working rabbi

他和創造奇蹟的教員


had never crossed paths.

從未相逢。


💠



And now -- it was too late.

現在——為時已晚。


When the Romans had crucified Jesus,

當羅馬人把耶穌釘在十字架上時，


hope had died in the lame man's heart.

瘸子心中的希望已經破滅。


He was over 40 now.

他現在已經40多歲了。


It was time to stop yearning

是時候停止渴望了


for what could never be.

為了永遠不可能發生的事情。


💠



Besides, two Jewish men -- potential donors -- were approaching the gate where he lay.

此外，兩名猶太男子——潛在的捐贈者——正走近他躺著的大門。


Asking for money was never easy, no matter how many times Azariah did it...

要求錢從來都不容易，不管亞撒利雅做了多少次......


but it had to be done.

但它必須完成。


Taking a deep breath...

深呼吸......


he held out his hand.

他伸出了手。


💠



Based on Acts 3:1-3; Acts 4:22.

基於使徒行傳3:1-3；使徒行傳4:22。


💠

item.title: 15 - Beyond words 無法用語言表達
item.description: Peter is amazed by the Holy Spirit 彼得對聖靈感到驚訝
item.content:encoded:
Peter was flabbergasted --

彼得驚呆了——


beyond words.

無法用語言表達。


The very thing he'd been terrified to do --

正是他害怕做的事情——


in failing his beloved Master the first time --

第一次辜負他心愛的主人——


the unschooled Galilean

這個未受教育的加利利人


had now done

現在已經完成了


before a vast crowd of educated Jews

在一大群受過教育的猶太人面前


in Jerusalem! --

在耶路撒冷！--


without the slightest hint of fear.

沒有絲毫的恐懼。


💠



In the crowd were many members of the murderous mob

人群中有許多殺人暴徒的成員


who -- demanding Jesus' death --

誰——要求處死耶穌——


had nearly rioted against the Romans.

差點對羅馬人發動暴亂。


Definitely not the easiest crowd to tell

絕對不是最容易告訴的人群


that He was alive --

他活著——


by any measure.

無論如何。


💠



Yet -- they had listened --

然而——他們聽了——


intently! --

專心致志！--


and responded!

並回應了！


Three thousand

三千個


remorseful, contrite hearts

懊悔，悔恨的心


baptized

受洗的


in the Name of the Father, the Son, and the Holy Spirit.

以聖父、聖子和聖靈的名義。


💠



This Holy Spirit

這個聖靈


was awesome --

太棒了——


beyond words.

無法用語言表達。


💠



Based on John 18:15-18,25-27; Acts 2:37-41.

基於約翰福音18:15-18,25-27；使徒行傳2:37-41。


💠

item.title: 14 - Proclamation through Peter 透過彼得宣佈
item.description: The Holy Spirit explains to the gathered crowd 聖靈向聚集的人群解釋
item.content:encoded:
"Men of Judea, and everyone living in Jerusalem!"

猶太人，以及住在耶路撒冷的每個人！


As Peter addressed the crowd,

當彼得向人群講話時，


words not his own

不是他自己的話


flowed effortlessly in Spirit streams from belly to tongue.

隨著聖靈的流動，從腹部毫不費力地流向舌頭。


"Know this, and hear my words!"

「知道這個，聽我的話！」


Every eye turned toward him.

每個人的目光都轉向了他。


💠



"These folks aren't drunk, as you suppose."

「這些人並沒有喝醉，儘管你可能認為他們喝醉了。」


Peter gestured toward his companions.

彼得向他的同伴們示意。


"It's only 9 am!"

「現在才上午9點！」


Smiles appeared here and there among the gathered onlookers.

聚集在圍觀者中，這裡和那裡出現了笑容。


"So...

「所以......


what is this, then?"

那這是什麼？」


He surveyed a sea of mystified eyes.

他望向一片充滿困惑的眼睛的海洋。


"This

「這個


is what was promised --

是承諾的——


through the prophet Joel:

透過先知約珥：


'In the last days,' says God, 'I pour out My Spirit on all flesh:

「在末世，」上帝說，「我把我的靈澆灌在所有有血氣的身上：


your sons and daughters prophesy;

你的兒女預言；


your young men see visions;

你的年輕人看到了異象；


your old men dream dreams.

你的老人做夢。


Also on My servants, both male and female, in those days,

還有我的僕人，包括那些日子的男性和女性，


I pour out My Spirit,

我傾注我的靈，


and they prophesy.'"

他們預言。'」


💠



Arshak's mind would have been completely boggled --

阿爾沙克的頭腦會完全困惑——


if it hadn't already been.

如果不是已經這樣的話。


The Galilean Jew

那個加利利猶太人


who had spoken Arshak's childhood dialect of Parthian --

誰說過阿爾沙克童年的帕提亞方言——


flawlessly in front of him! --

在他面前完美無瑕！——


was now speaking boldly in Aramaic to the entire crowd,

現在正用阿拉姆語向全體人群大膽地講話，


declaring, in no uncertain terms, that the recently crucified, miracle-working rabbi

毫不含糊地宣佈，這位最近被釘在十字架上的、創造奇蹟的教員


was alive --

活著——


had risen

已經復活了


from death! --

從死亡中！--


and they -- His followers -- had seen Him!

他們——他的追隨者——見過他！


💠



The Galilean's startling claims culminated in a calm, clear assertion:

加利利人令人震驚的主張最終導致了一個平靜、明確的斷言：


"Therefore, let all Israel be confident

「因此，讓所有以色列人毫無疑問地知道


that God has made

上帝已任命


both Lord and Messiah

主和彌賽亞


this same Jesus --

這位耶穌——


whom you crucified."

你釘在十字架上的人。」


💠



Based on Joel 2:28-29; Acts 2:14-36.

基於約珥書2:28-29；使徒行傳2:14-36。


💠

item.title: 13 - Open hearts 敞開心扉
item.description: The Holy Spirit revels 聖靈歡喜
item.content:encoded:
The Holy Spirit was having a delightful time

聖靈正在度過一段愉快的時光


declaring the Father's deeds in a dozen different languages -- the mother tongues of the strangers in the gathered crowd --

用十幾種不同的語言——聚集的人群中外國人的母語——宣佈天父的事蹟——


enjoying their amazement,

享受他們的驚奇，


as hearts opened.

心扉敞開。


💠



But others -- who did not recognize any of the languages being spoken -- began to laugh and jeer.

但其他人——他們不認識所說的任何語言——開始大笑和嘲弄。


"They're full of new wine -- drunk!"

「他們喝滿了新酒——喝醉了！」


The crowd was fully gathered;

人群完全聚集了；


it was time to explain.

是時候解釋了。


💠



As the other Apostles fell silent,

當其他使徒們沉默時，


the wonderful rush of unknown languages through their bellies to their tongues

未知語言透過肚子進入舌頭的奇妙衝刺


tapering

漸漸減少的


to mere streams of living waters --

到活水的溪流——


Peter sensed a convergence of Holy Spirit inflow...

彼得感受到聖靈的湧入正在匯聚......


and suddenly knew just what to say.

突然知道該說什麼了。


💠



Based on Acts 2:1-14.

基於使徒行傳2:1-14。


💠

item.title: 12 - Arshak's dialect 阿爾沙克方言
item.description: A Parthian hears his mother tongue 一個帕提亞人聽他的母語
item.content:encoded:
Arshak couldn't believe his ears.

阿爾沙克不敢相信自己的耳朵。


The young Gentile convert to the Jewish faith had come all the way to Judea from Parthia, his homeland, to celebrate the Passover.

這位皈依猶太教的年輕非猶太人從他的家鄉帕提亞一路來到猶地亞慶祝逾越節。


Although the feast had ended

雖然盛宴已經結束了


well over a month ago --

一個多月前——


he'd stayed on in Jerusalem.

他一直呆在耶路撒冷。


For some reason, he didn't feel ready to leave...

出於某種原因，他還沒有準備好離開......


not yet.

還沒有。


💠



In Jerusalem, the young Parthian had met Jews from all over Israel:

在耶路撒冷，年輕的帕提亞人遇到了來自以色列各地的猶太人：


Judea, the Jordan, Galilee...

猶太、約旦、加利利......


and Jews and other Gentile converts from all over the world:

以及來自世界各地的猶太人和其他外邦人皈依者：


Elam, Media, Asia, Egypt, Rome, Arabia... and more!

伊拉姆、媒體、亞洲、埃及、羅馬、阿拉伯......以及更多！


The list seemed endless.

名單似乎無窮無盡。


💠



It had been a very unusual -- actually disturbing -- Passover this year...!

今年的逾越節非常不尋常——實際上令人不安......！


A miracle-working rabbi --

一位創造奇蹟的教員——


who'd claimed to be a "king"...

誰自稱是「國王」......


even... the Son of God!

甚至......上帝之子！


(and broken the Sabbath laws) --

（並違反了安息日的法律）——


had been cruelly tortured and crucified by the Romans...

被羅馬人殘忍地折磨和釘在十字架上......


at the insistence of the Jews.

在猶太人的堅持下。


Then, several days later, rumours had spread that the same rabbi's followers had stolen His body!

然後，幾天後，謠言傳開了，說同一個教員的追隨者偷了他的屍體！


Arshak had been troubled and intrigued by these strange incidents...

阿爾沙克對這些奇怪的事件感到困擾和好奇......


perhaps the reason he still hadn't returned home.

也許這就是他還沒有回家的原因。


💠



To relate to the locals better, Arshak had learned to speak Aramaic passably well.

為了更好地與當地人建立聯絡，阿爾沙克學會了說好亞拉姆語。


This was the language the Jews spoke among themselves --

這是猶太人之間所說的語言——


with some regional variations... like in Galilee.

有一些地區差異......比如在加利利。


So --

所以——


why was there a Galilean Jew, standing right in front of him, telling him of wonderful things God had done --

為什麼有一個加利利猶太人站在他面前，告訴他上帝所做的奇妙事情——


not in the standard Aramaic of Jerusalem, nor in the Galilean dialect,

不是耶路撒冷的標準阿拉姆語，也不是加利利方言，


nor even in Hebrew or Greek! --

甚至連希伯來文或希臘文都不會！ ——


but in Parthian -- Arshak's own mother tongue --

但在帕提亞語中——阿爾沙克自己的母語——


even... the very dialect he had learned

甚至......他學過的方言


as a child?!

小時候？！


💠



Based on Acts 2:1-12.

基於使徒行傳2:1-12。


💠

item.title: 11 - The big wind 大風
item.description: Isaac hears the big wind blow 艾薩克聽到大風吹來
item.content:encoded:
Isaac was playing outside with the other kids when the big noise came --

艾薩克正在外面和其他孩子玩耍，突然傳來一陣巨大的噪音——


like a whooshing wind that kept on going for a while.

就像持續了一段時間的嗖嗖風。


As soon as it stopped, he ran to the house and up the steps as fast as he could --

風一停，他就跑回屋裡，以最快的速度跑上樓梯——


to where Mommy and Daddy had been praying with all the others.

去爸爸媽媽和其他人一起祈禱的地方。


Everyone was standing, milling about, each saying something --

每個人都站著，四處遊蕩，各自說著什麼——


something Isaac couldn't understand.

艾薩克無法明白的事情。


💠



He spotted Mommy, and ran to her.

他發現了媽媽，然後跑向她。


What was that fire on top of her head?!

她頭頂上的火是什麼？！


She bent down to him with a big, happy smile, scooping him up lovingly in her arms as she kept saying... something...

她彎下腰向他，帶著燦爛的、快樂的微笑，充滿愛意地把他抱在懷裡，她不停地說......某事......


Isaac didn't know what.

艾薩克不知道那是什麼。


💠



Mommy carried Isaac out of the house and down the steps, following all the others --

媽媽把艾薩克抱出家門，跟著其他人走下臺階——


they all had a fire on top of their heads, too!

他們的頭頂也著火了！


A great, big crowd of people -- without any fire! -- had gathered outside.

一大群人——沒有火！——聚集在外面。


They had heard the big, whooshing wind sound, too, and wanted to know what was going on.

他們也聽到了那巨大的嗖嗖風聲，想知道發生了什麼。


But Isaac couldn't tell them.

但艾薩克無法告訴他們。


He didn't know, either!

他也不知道！


💠



Based on Acts 1:12-14; Acts 2:1-6.

基於使徒行傳1:12-14；使徒行傳2:1-6。


💠

item.title: 10 - Time 時候
item.description: The Holy Spirit is ready 聖靈已經準備好了
item.content:encoded:
All of the disciples were sitting together in the upper room, praying, waiting, reminiscing...

所有的門徒都坐在樓上，祈禱、等待、回憶......


The Holy Spirit counted 126 men -- all Jesus' chosen Apostles, all who had followed Him here to Jerusalem from Galilee, including His brothers, and from other regions of Israel -- plus His mother and the other women, and a few of the children who were not outside playing...

聖靈數了數，一共有 126 個名男——耶穌所揀選的所有使徒，所有跟隨他從加利利到耶路撒冷的人，包括他的兄弟和來自以色列其他地區的人——再加上他的母親和其他女人，以及一些沒有在外面玩耍的孩子……


although most were.

儘管大多數孩子都在戶外。


💠



And now,

現在，


at last,

最後，


it was time.

是時候了。


Time to reverse the effects of Babel.

是時候扭轉巴別塔影響了。


After so many millennia,

經過這麼多千年，


the Holy Spirit was pumped.

聖靈很興奮，準備好了。


💠



Based on Acts 1:12-14; Acts 2:1-4.

基於使徒行傳1:12-14；使徒行傳2:1-4。


💠

item.title: 9 - The Tower 塔
item.description: The Holy Spirit remembers Babel 聖靈記得巴別
item.content:encoded:
In the ancient past, before the earth was divided, not many generations after the great Flood...

在古代，在分地之前，在大洪水之後的幾代人......


all surviving humanity spoke the same language.

所有倖存的人類都說同樣的語言。


These descendants of Noah agreed to work together

這些諾亞的後代同意一起工作


to build a city, with a tower soaring to the Heavens,

建造一座城市，一座塔高聳入雲，


make a name for themselves,

為自己以揚名立姓，


and not be dispersed over the whole earth.

並且不會分散在整個地球上。


💠



The tower construction was so impressive, the Father Himself came down, together with the Son and Holy Spirit, to see it.

這座塔的建造令人印象深刻，天父本人與聖子和聖靈一起下來觀看。


Once they determined that this project -- and whatever else humanity, working together, decided to accomplish -- would be successful...

一旦他們確定這個專案——以及人類共同努力，決定完成的任何其他事情——都會成功......


the Father spoke.

父親說話了。


"We must garble their communication."

「我們必須混淆他們的溝通。」


💠



That day, every family on earth received a new language, causing mass confusion among the builders.

那天，地球上的每個家庭都被賦予了一種新語言，這在建造者中造成了大規模的混亂。


All collaboration on the construction was abandoned.

所有關於建築的合作都被放棄了。


In human history, the unfinished structure came to be known as "the Tower of Babel."

在人類歷史上，這座未完成的建築被稱為「巴別塔」。


💠



At that time, the Father separated the families into peoples, and sent each people to live in separate territories, scattered throughout the earth...

那時，天父將所有的家庭分成不同的民族，並將每個民族分散到不同的地區居住，散佈到世界各地......


the Holy Spirit remembered.

聖靈記住了。


💠



Based on Genesis 6:5 - Genesis 10:32; Genesis 11:1-9.

基於創世記6:5-創世記10:32；創世記11:1-9。


💠

item.title: 8 - Sent 派遣的
item.description: The Father dispatches the Holy Spirit 天父派遣聖靈
item.content:encoded:
Eight or nine earth days later, after a scintillating tour of the cosmos, Jesus arrived home to a joyous welcome.

地球八、九天後，在一次精彩化的宇宙之旅後，耶穌回到家裡，受到了熱烈的歡迎。


The harmonious, interweaving melodies of angelic praise were heavenly --

和諧、交織的天使讚美旋律是天堂般的——


but it was the look of approval on His Father's face that made all the Son had suffered...

但父親臉上讚許的表情讓兒子感受到他所遭受的一切......


truly worthwhile.

真的很值得。


💠



After He'd settled into the throne beside His Father, Jesus turned and spoke quietly to Him.

在他坐在父親身邊的寶座上後，耶穌轉過身來，悄悄地對他說話。


The Father nodded in agreement,

父親點頭表示同意，


then turned to the Holy Spirit, who was standing before them, waiting patiently.

然後轉向站在他們面前的聖靈，耐心地等待著。


"You're up."

「輪到你了。」


💠



Based on Mark 16:19; John 14:15-17; John 16:5-7.

基於馬可福音16:19；約翰福音14:15-17；約翰福音16:5-7。


💠

item.title: 7 - Angelic dismissal 天使送離開人群
item.description: The angels dismiss the disciples 天使遣散門徒
item.content:encoded:
Just before leaving the cover of the clouds, Gabriel and Raphael checked to make sure they were invisible.

在離開雲層之前，加百列和拉斐爾檢查了一下，以確保他們是隱形的。


Now descending unseen, they saw a crowd of about 120 men, along with numerous women and children, all staring up at them --

現在在隱形地情況下下降，他們看到大約120名男子的人群，以及許多婦女和兒童，都盯著他們——


not really at them, of course, because they were invisible --

當然，不是真的對他們，因為他們是看不見的——


but at the clouds, which had hidden their Master from sight.

而是在看阻擋他們再也看不見主的雲。


💠



The two angels dropped down lightly a distance behind the disciples, so as not to cause a panic.

兩位天使輕輕地降落在門徒身後一段距離，以免引起恐慌。


All in the heavenly realms -- both faithful and fallen -- were well-aware of mortals' peculiar propensity to terror.

所有在天堂領域——無論是忠實的還是墮落的——都深知凡人特別容易受到恐懼。


Gabriel -- then Raphael -- turned on visibility, so they could be seen -- excepting their wings, of course! -- and quietly joined the crowd.

加百列——然後是拉斐爾——開啟能見度，這樣就可以看到他們了——當然，除了他們的翅膀！——然後悄悄地加入了人群。


Then Gabriel spoke clearly:

然後加百列清晰地說：


"Men of Galilee, why do you stand here, gazing into the sky? This Jesus, who went up from you into Heaven, will return in the same way you saw Him leave."

「加利利人，你們為什麼站在這裡，凝視著天空？ 這位從你那裡升到天堂的耶穌，將以你看到他離開的方式回來。」


💠



Based on Acts 1:9-14.

基於使徒行傳1:9-14。


💠

item.title: 6 - Triumphal Tour 勝利之旅
item.description: Jesus travels around the Universe 耶穌環遊宇宙
item.content:encoded:
As He broke through the tops of the clouds, Jesus spied Gabriel and Raphael waiting expectantly for their cue.

當耶穌衝破雲層時，他看到加百列和拉斐爾滿懷期待地等待著他們的暗示。


"You'd better go now," He advised them with a twinkle in His eye. "Don't want'em to get a crick in their necks."

「你最好現在就走，」他眼睛裡閃爍著眼睛勸告他們。 「不想讓他們因為抬頭太久而傷到脖子。」


The two angels grinned, saluted their King, and dropped gladly down through the clouds.

兩個天使咧嘴一笑，向他們的國王敬禮，然後高興地從雲層中落下。


💠



As Jesus toured the Universe on His way home, He reminisced.

當耶穌在回家的路上遊覽宇宙時，他回憶了。


He was relieved to have completed His undeniably difficult assignment -- the master plan He and His Father had devised together, long before creating the cosmos -- and with it, the Earth.

他鬆了一口氣，因為他完成了不可否認的艱鉅任務——他和他的父親就共同製定了這個總體規劃，早在他們創造宇宙和地球之前。


Now all that remained was a triumphal tour of the Universe -- and a seat at His Father's right hand in Heaven.

現在，他所要做的就是在宇宙中進行一次凱旋之旅——然後在天堂裡坐在他父親的右手邊。


The triumphal tour was optional, of course, but --

當然，凱旋之旅是可選的，但是——


Jesus always enjoyed visiting with the celestial beings...

耶穌總是喜歡拜訪天人......


known on earth as mere stars...

在地球上被稱為恆星......


They had such fascinating personalities.

他們的個性非常迷人。


💠



As He flew on, Jesus thought about His chosen Apostles and disciples.

當他飛翔時，耶穌想到了他揀選的使徒和門徒。


He hadn't been terribly surprised at the question that had revealed their continuing focus on conquest, despite His best efforts to show them a better way.

儘管他盡了最大努力向他們展示更好的方法，但他們提出的問題表明他們仍然專注於征服，他並不感到非常驚訝。


So He had replied, gently and patiently,

所以他溫柔而耐心地回答道，


"It is not for you to know the times or seasons the Father has appointed by His own authority --

「你們不必知道父憑著自己的權柄所定的時間或季節——


but you will receive power when the Holy Spirit comes upon you, and you will be My

但當聖靈降臨在你身上時，你將得到力量，你將成為我的


witnesses

證人


in Jerusalem, in all Judea and Samaria, and to the ends of the earth."

在耶路撒冷，在整個猶太和撒瑪利亞，一直到天涯海角。」


He hoped they'd gotten it this time.

他希望這次他們能夠理解。


💠



Based on Mark 16:19; Luke 24:49-51; Acts 1:1-11.

基於馬可福音16:19；路加福音24:49-51；使徒行傳1:1-11。


💠

item.title: 5 - Waiting: James 等待：詹姆斯
item.description: James remembers Jesus ascending to Heaven 詹姆斯記得耶穌昇天
item.content:encoded:
James sat waiting and praying with the other Apostles.

詹姆斯和其他使徒們一起坐著等待和祈禱。


The past three years had been the most eventful -- heart-rending -- and joyous! -- of his entire life.

過去的三年是他整個一生中最多事的——令人心碎——和快樂！


It was a relief now to have this time to be silent, meditate and pray, as they waited for the Master's promise to be fulfilled.

現在有這個時間安靜、冥想和祈禱，當他們等待師父的承諾實現時，真是鬆了一口氣。


💠



The day Jesus had gone back to Heaven, James had been expecting He would free Israel from the Roman oppressors. He knew many -- if not all! -- of the other Apostles had felt the same.

詹姆斯回想起耶穌升天的那一天。那一天，詹姆斯一直希望並期待耶穌能將以色列從羅馬壓迫者手中解放出來。 他知道許多——如果不是全部的話！——其他使徒也有同樣的感覺。


So when Jesus had commanded them to remain in Jerusalem until they were "clothed with power from on high," he'd imagined it was so they might establish Jesus as the nation's king.

因此，當耶穌命令他們留在耶路撒冷，直到他們「披上從上而來的權力」時，詹姆斯想像他們這樣做是為了加冕耶穌為國家的國王。


Although... James reflected now...

雖然... 詹姆斯現在反思了......


Most of the Master's teachings were about being "poor in spirit"... gentle... kind... like a child... a servant... loving their enemies...

大師的大部分教義是關於「精神貧乏」......溫柔......善良......像個孩子......一個僕人......愛他們的敵人......


He didn't quite see how it all fit together.

他不太明白這一切是如何結合在一起的。


💠



He hadn't been the least bit surprised when one of the other Apostles -- probably Peter! -- had asked Jesus bluntly:

彼得很可能是直接向耶穌提出這個問題的使徒，而詹姆斯對此一點也不感到驚訝。


"Lord, is it time now for You to restore the kingdom to Israel?"

「主啊，現在是你恢復以色列王國的時候嗎？」


It was what James himself had been wondering, ever since Jesus had come back to life.

自從耶穌復活以來，詹姆斯自己一直想知道這一點。


💠



But...

但是...


he had been completely taken by surprise when Jesus' feet had left the earth and He'd started floating higher and higher, up into the sky.

當耶穌的腳離開地面時，他完全驚呆了。然後耶穌開始在空中飄得越來越高。


Where was their King going?!

他們的國王要去哪裡？！


💠



Based on Mark 16:19; Luke 24:49-53; Acts 1:1-14.

基於馬可福音16:19；路加福音24:49-53；使徒行傳1:1-14。


💠

item.title: 4 - Waiting: Mary 等待：馬利亞
item.description: Mary remembers the angel Gabriel 馬利亞記得天使加百列
item.content:encoded:
Mary, Jesus' mother, sat waiting and praying with the rest of her Son's followers.

耶穌的母親馬利亞和她兒子的其他追隨者一起坐著等待和祈禱。


Whenever there was a practical need, she or one of the other women gladly took care of it.

每當有實際需求時，她或其他女性都會欣然處理。


Since her firstborn -- Jesus -- had been taken up to Heaven eight days ago, she was grateful still to have a role among the disciples.

由於她的長子——耶穌——八天前被飛升天堂，她很慶幸自己仍然能夠幫助他的弟子。


💠



When Jesus had led them all up to the top of a high mountain, His mother had already had an inkling of what was about to happen.

當耶穌帶領他們所有人登上一座高山時，他的母親已經預感到即將發生的事情。


Her Son had always been loving and attentive toward her, but that morning He'd been especially kind...

她的兒子一直對她充滿愛和關心，但那天早上他特別善良......


sad... yet joyful, at the same time.

悲傷......但同時也令人高興。


His warm embrace, before leading them all out, had told Mary all she needed to know...

在他帶領他們所有人出去之前，他熱情的擁抱告訴了馬利亞她需要知道的一切......


It was time.

是時候了。


Her firstborn Son was returning to His Father.

她的長子要回到他父親那裡了。


💠



While waiting with the others for the Holy Spirit to come, Mary thought about the first angel she had ever met, puzzling over his message.

在和其他人一起等待聖靈降臨時，馬利亞想到了她遇到的第一個天使，嘗試理解他的訊息。


Gabriel had come from God to tell her about the Son which she, a virgin, would miraculously conceive:

加百列從上帝那裡來，告訴她一個奇蹟，她將會生下一個兒子，儘管她還是處女：


"He will be great and will be called the Son of the Most High. The Lord God will give Him the throne of His father, David. He will reign over the house of Jacob forever, and His kingdom will never end.”
